cmd/indexion/plan/solid/cli.mbt::value:f.name: find_cross_matches
cmd/indexion/plan/solid/cli.mbt::value:f.doc:
Find cross-directory matches above threshold using content-based comparison.
 Phase 1: path-based matches (identical relative paths — high confidence).
 Phase 2: content-based matches (different names but similar content).

cmd/indexion/plan/solid/cli.mbt::value:f.name: find_cross_function_matches
cmd/indexion/plan/solid/cli.mbt::value:f.doc:
Find cross-directory function-level duplicates.
 Extracts functions from all files via KGF grammar pipeline
 (tokenize→parse→eval_semantics→declares edges), compares function bodies
 via TF-IDF, and returns only cross-directory pairs.

cmd/indexion/plan/solid/cli.mbt::value:a.name: add_function_body
cmd/indexion/plan/solid/cli.mbt::value:a.doc:
Add a function body to the extraction arrays.
 Shared by both token-based and grammar-based extraction paths.

cmd/indexion/plan/solid/cli.mbt::value:b.name: build_extraction_tasks
cmd/indexion/plan/solid/cli.mbt::value:b.doc:
Group cross-function matches by shared function name/signature.
 Returns extraction tasks grouped by target, with functions to extract.

cmd/indexion/plan/solid/cli.mbt::value:r.name: resolve_extraction_target
cmd/indexion/plan/solid/cli.mbt::value:r.doc: Resolve extraction target for a function using rules or default.

cmd/indexion/plan/solid/cli.mbt::value:r.name: resolve_target
cmd/indexion/plan/solid/cli.mbt::value:r.doc: Resolve target path (handle @ prefix for absolute).

cmd/indexion/plan/refactor/plan_json.mbt.doc: Trim leading whitespace from text.

Hint message for when a digest index is required but not found.
 SoT for the build command suggestion across all consumers (search, serve, etc.).

cmd/indexion/digest/cli.mbt::value:log_e.name: log_error
cmd/indexion/digest/cli.mbt::value:log_e.doc: Write error message (TODO: use stderr when available).

cmd/indexion/digest/cli.mbt::value:resol.name: resolve_provider
cmd/indexion/digest/cli.mbt::value:resol.doc:
Resolve embedding provider from CLI config.
 Delegates to @embed_config.resolve_provider (SoT for provider resolution).
 dim is adjusted: if still at TF-IDF default and provider=openai, use OPENAI_EMBEDDING_DIM.
 Raises String with an error message if the provider cannot be resolved.

cmd/indexion/digest/cli.mbt::value:is_pr.name: is_provider_available
cmd/indexion/digest/cli.mbt::value:is_pr.doc:

 Returns false when openai was requested but API key is missing.

cmd/indexion/digest/cli.mbt::value:build.doc:
Build graph from source directory.
 Returns both the CodeGraph and a map of file content hashes for accurate
 change detection in incremental index updates.

cmd/indexion/digest/cli.mbt::value:detec.name: detect_source_dir
cmd/indexion/digest/cli.mbt::value:detec.doc:
Detect source directory for auto-update.
 Priority: --source flag > input_path from build > "." (current directory).

cmd/indexion/digest/cli.mbt::value:run_s.doc: Show index status including pending changes.

cmd/indexion/segment/cli.mbt::value:comm.doc: Define the segment command for argparse.

cmd/indexion/segment/cli.mbt::value:run_.name: run_with_config
cmd/indexion/segment/cli.mbt::value:run_.doc: Run the segment command with a parsed config.

cmd/indexion/spec/cli.mbt::value:command.name: command
cmd/indexion/spec/cli.mbt::value:command.doc: Build the argparse Command for the spec subcommand.

cmd/indexion/spec/verify/cli.mbt::value:.name: run_to_string
cmd/indexion/spec/verify/cli.mbt::value:.doc: Run spec verify analysis and return rendered output as a string.

cmd/indexion/spec/verify/render.mbt::val.name: render_output
cmd/indexion/spec/verify/render.mbt::val.doc: Render the verify report in the requested format.

cmd/indexion/spec/verify/render.mbt::val.name: render_github_issue
cmd/indexion/spec/verify/render.mbt::val.doc: Render as GitHub issue format with checkboxes.

cmd/indexion/spec/verify/render.mbt::val.name: collect_unique_kinds

cmd/indexion/spec/verify/types.mbt::valu.name: default
cmd/indexion/spec/verify/types.mbt::valu.doc: Create a default verify configuration with sensible defaults.

cmd/indexion/spec/verify/types.mbt::valu.name: SpecSymbol
cmd/indexion/spec/verify/types.mbt::valu.doc:
A symbol (gap term) identified in spec but missing from implementation.
 `kind` is derived from the KGF token type (e.g., "ident", "text", "vocab").

cmd/indexion/spec/verify/types.mbt::valu.name: ImplEvidence
cmd/indexion/spec/verify/types.mbt::valu.doc: Evidence for the gap term finding.

cmd/indexion/spec/verify/types.mbt::valu.name: VerifySummary
cmd/indexion/spec/verify/types.mbt::valu.doc: Summary statistics for the verify report.

cmd/indexion/spec/verify/types.mbt::valu.name: VerifyReportConfig

cmd/indexion/similarity/cli.mbt::value:c.name: command
cmd/indexion/similarity/cli.mbt::value:c.doc: Define the similarity command for argparse.

cmd/indexion/similarity/cli.mbt::value:c.name: calculate_similarity
cmd/indexion/similarity/cli.mbt::value:c.doc:
Calculate similarity using the specified strategy.
 Uses StrategyKind from src/similarity as SoT.

cmd/indexion/similarity/cli.mbt::value:p.name: pad_left
cmd/indexion/similarity/cli.mbt::value:p.doc: Pad string to left with character.

cmd/indexion/wiki/hook/cli.mbt::value:bu.name: build_post_commit_script
cmd/indexion/wiki/hook/cli.mbt::value:bu.doc: Runs `indexion wiki pages ingest` for every commit.

cmd/indexion/wiki/hook/cli.mbt::value:bu.name: build_post_checkout_script
cmd/indexion/wiki/hook/cli.mbt::value:bu.doc:
Runs `indexion wiki pages ingest` only on branch switches (not file checkouts).
 In POSIX shell hooks, $3 == 1 signals a branch switch; $3 == 0 is a file checkout.

cmd/indexion/wiki/hook/cli.mbt::value:un.name: uninstall_hook
cmd/indexion/wiki/hook/cli.mbt::value:un.doc:
Remove the indexion-managed section from a hook file.
 If only a shebang remains, the file is deleted.

cmd/indexion/wiki/hook/cli.mbt::value:ho.name: hook_status
cmd/indexion/wiki/hook/cli.mbt::value:ho.doc: Return "installed" or "not installed" for a hook file.

cmd/indexion/wiki/hook/cli.mbt::value:se.name: section_installed
cmd/indexion/wiki/hook/cli.mbt::value:se.doc:
Return true if `content` already contains the indexion start marker.
 Uses the KGF shell lexer to identify comment tokens precisely.

cmd/indexion/wiki/hook/cli.mbt::value:re.name: remove_hook_section
cmd/indexion/wiki/hook/cli.mbt::value:re.doc:
Remove the indexion-managed section from `content` using the KGF shell
 lexer to locate the marker comment tokens.
 Returns `content` unchanged if no markers are found.

cmd/indexion/wiki/hook/cli.mbt::value:fi.name: find_marker_range
cmd/indexion/wiki/hook/cli.mbt::value:fi.doc:
Locate the start/end character positions of the indexion section.

 Scans KGF `Comment` tokens for the start and end markers.
 Returns `Some((section_start, section_end))` where:
   - `section_start` is the position of the first character of the start marker line
   - `section_end`   is the position just after the end marker line (newline included)

cmd/indexion/wiki/hook/cli.mbt::value:sh.name: shell_comment_tokens
cmd/indexion/wiki/hook/cli.mbt::value:sh.doc:
Tokenize `content` as a POSIX shell script using the KGF shell lexer
 and return only the `Comment` tokens.

cmd/indexion/wiki/hook/cli.mbt::value:sh.name: shell_token_defs
cmd/indexion/wiki/hook/cli.mbt::value:sh.doc:
Token definitions for the KGF shell lexer — mirrors `kgfs/dsl/shell.kgf`.
 Kept in sync with the KGF spec so that the lexer correctly handles quoting
 and escaping before we scan for marker comments.

cmd/indexion/wiki/hook/cli.mbt::value:is.name: is_shebang_only
cmd/indexion/wiki/hook/cli.mbt::value:is.doc: Return true if `content` has no meaningful content beyond an optional shebang.

cmd/indexion/mcp/tools.mbt::value:build_.name: build_server
cmd/indexion/mcp/tools.mbt::value:build_.doc: Build an MCP server with all indexion tools registered.

cmd/indexion/grep/pattern.mbt::value:Tok.name: TokenMatcher
cmd/indexion/grep/pattern.mbt::value:Tok.doc: A single token matcher in a pattern.

cmd/indexion/grep/pattern.mbt::value:res.name: resolve_alias
cmd/indexion/grep/pattern.mbt::value:res.doc:
Resolve a single pattern part using the alias table.
 If the part matches an alias (e.g. "pub" → "KW_pub"), return the token kind.
 Otherwise return the part unchanged.

cmd/indexion/grep/search.mbt::value:load.doc: Load KGF registry and collect files, or print error and return None.

cmd/indexion/explore/cli.mbt::value:buil.name: build_json

cmd/indexion/explore/cli.mbt::value:calc.doc:
Calculate pairs using @batch.compare for strategies that have

cmd/indexion/search/corpus.mbt::value:re.name: resolve_wiki_path
cmd/indexion/search/corpus.mbt::value:re.doc: Resolve wiki path — if it's `.indexion/wiki`, resolve via config.

cmd/indexion/common/args.mbt::value:is_d.name: is_directory
cmd/indexion/common/args.mbt::value:is_d.doc: Check if path is a directory.

cmd/indexion/common/args.mbt::value:find.name: find_json_key_position

cmd/indexion/doc/readme/render.mbt::valu.name: render_raw

cmd/indexion/serve/cli.mbt::value:Server.doc: Mutable server state — replaced atomically on rebuild.

cmd/indexion/serve/cli.mbt::value:build_.name: build_get_routes
cmd/indexion/serve/cli.mbt::value:build_.doc:
Build a lookup table of all GET API routes: path -> JSON response data.
 This is the **single source of truth** for API paths. Used by:
   - HTTP server: lookup on incoming GET requests
   - `serve export`: iterate to write static files
 Paths include the /api prefix to match incoming request paths directly.

cmd/indexion/serve/export.mbt::value:wri.doc: Write a string to a file, creating parent directories as needed.

cmd/indexion/serve/api_handlers.mbt::val.doc: Validate that a path does not contain path traversal sequences.

cmd/indexion/serve/api_handlers.mbt::val.doc: Extract a string field from parsed JSON, with a default.

cmd/indexion/serve/api_handlers.mbt::val.name: json_double
cmd/indexion/serve/api_handlers.mbt::val.doc: Extract a double field from parsed JSON, with a default.

cmd/indexion/serve/api_handlers.mbt::val.name: json_string_array
cmd/indexion/serve/api_handlers.mbt::val.doc: Extract a string array from parsed JSON, with a default.

cmd/indexion/serve/api_handlers.mbt::val.name: json_bool
cmd/indexion/serve/api_handlers.mbt::val.doc: Extract a bool field from parsed JSON, with a default.

cmd/indexion/serve/api_handlers.mbt::val.name: build_config_json
cmd/indexion/serve/api_handlers.mbt::val.doc: Build readonly config JSON from ServeConfig.

cmd/indexion/perf/cli.mbt::value:command.name: command
cmd/indexion/perf/cli.mbt::value:command.doc: Define the perf command with kgf subcommand for argparse.

cmd/kgf-tokenizer/main.mbt::value:tokeni.name: tokenize
cmd/kgf-tokenizer/main.mbt::value:tokeni.doc:
Tokenize source code using a KGF spec string.
 Returns a JSON string: [{"kind":"...","text":"...","pos":N}, ...]

tests/integration/cross-package-resoluti.name: analyze_fixture
tests/integration/cross-package-resoluti.doc:
Analyze a fixture directory and return the resulting CodeGraph. The test
 caller passes the relative path to the fixture from the repo root; the
 analyzer is invoked identically to the `doc graph` CLI.

tests/integration/cross-package-resoluti.name: assert_snapshot_matches
tests/integration/cross-package-resoluti.doc:
Compare `actual` against the contents of `snapshot_path`. On mismatch,
 either update the snapshot (if `INDEXION_UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1` is set) or
 fail with a diff-friendly error message.

packages/graph-canvas/src/layout/hierarc.name: LEAF_BASE_RADIUS
packages/graph-canvas/src/layout/hierarc.doc:
Minimum radius of a leaf cluster (a single-node shell is about
 *  this wide). All sizing is relative to this.

packages/graph-canvas/src/layout/hierarc.name: SIBLING_PADDING
packages/graph-canvas/src/layout/hierarc.doc:
Extra world-units of padding between sibling clusters so they
 *  don't kiss.

packages/graph-canvas/src/layout/hierarc.name: OWNERSHIP_SHELL_SCALE
packages/graph-canvas/src/layout/hierarc.doc:
Radius granted to a mid-level path for its own owned nodes,
 *  relative to the sibling slot size.

packages/graph-canvas/src/layout/hierarc.name: shellRadiusFor
packages/graph-canvas/src/layout/hierarc.doc:
* Radius of a simple shell holding `count` nodes. Uses the sphere
 * packing rule r_shell ≈ LEAF_BASE_RADIUS · √count, softened at the
 * low end so a single node still occupies a visible area.

packages/graph-canvas/src/layout/layout..name: sampleGraph
packages/graph-canvas/src/layout/layout..doc:
A small graph with folder-like node ids so the hierarchical path
 *  clustering has something to work with.

packages/graph-canvas/src/layout/layout..name: snapshotPositions
packages/graph-canvas/src/layout/layout..doc:
Record each node's current (x, y, z) so we can compare after a
 *  layout pass.

packages/graph-canvas/src/clustering/ind.name: byCommunity
packages/graph-canvas/src/clustering/ind.doc:
* Modularity-greedy community detection (Louvain phase 1).
 *
 * For each node, we compute the modularity gain from moving it into
 * each neighbouring community, and choose the best. Iterated until
 * stable. This typically produces balanced mid-sized communities
 * rather than one giant blob (which plain label propagation tends to).
 *
 * Modularity gain formula (undirected):
 *   ΔQ = [ k_in / m ]  −  [ Σ_tot · k / (2 m²) ]
 * where k_in is the sum of edge weights from the node to nodes already
 * in the target community, Σ_tot is the total edge weight incident on
 * the target community, k is the degree of the node, and m is the
 * total edge weight in the graph.

packages/mermaid-viewer/src/mermaid-diag.name: parseMermaidSvg
packages/mermaid-viewer/src/mermaid-diag.doc: Parse mermaid SVG: ensure viewBox, set intrinsic w/h, strip inline style.

packages/mermaid-viewer/src/mermaid-diag.name: useMermaidSvg
packages/mermaid-viewer/src/mermaid-diag.doc: Lazy-load mermaid, render code to normalized SVG data.

packages/mermaid-viewer/src/mermaid-diag.name: useFitToWidth
packages/mermaid-viewer/src/mermaid-diag.doc:
* Fit-to-width state tracked via a monotonic version counter.
 * `svgVersion` increments when svg changes (not yet fitted).
 * `fitVersion` increments when fitToWidth completes.
 * `ready` = they match (current svg has been fitted).

packages/wiki/vite.config.ts::value:arti.name: artifactPath
packages/wiki/vite.config.ts::value:arti.doc:
* When `moon build --target js` has been run, the MoonBit-compiled
 * tokenizer is available. Alias @indexion/kgf-tokenizer to it for
 * real syntax highlighting. Otherwise the package's default stub
 * export is used (returns empty tokens → plain text rendering).

packages/wiki/src/client/lib/api-cache.t.name: SCOPES
packages/wiki/src/client/lib/api-cache.t.doc: Invalidation scopes. `invalidateScope("digest")` clears all digest keys.

packages/wiki/src/client/lib/kind-colors.name: PALETTE_BADGE
packages/wiki/src/client/lib/kind-colors.doc:
* @file Dynamic kind-based color assignment.
 *
 * Assigns colors to symbol kinds from a fixed palette, but does NOT
 * hardcode which kind gets which color. Colors are assigned in
 * encounter order and cached for consistency within a session.

packages/wiki/src/client/pages/explorer/.name: pruneTree
packages/wiki/src/client/pages/explorer/.doc:
* LOD: collapse leaf nodes under a common parent when there are too many.
 * Returns a pruned set of nodes and edges suitable for readable mermaid output.

packages/wiki/src/client/pages/explorer/.name: treeToMermaid
packages/wiki/src/client/pages/explorer/.doc: Build mermaid code from a GraphTree, using module paths as labels.

packages/api-client/src/sse.ts::value:YI.name: YIELD_BATCH_SIZE
packages/api-client/src/sse.ts::value:YI.doc: Max events to emit before yielding to avoid blocking the event loop.

packages/syntax-highlight/src/use-kgf-hi.name: ensureSpec
packages/syntax-highlight/src/use-kgf-hi.doc:
* Fetch spec for lang (with universal fallback), update cache,
 * and notify subscribers. Safe to call multiple times — deduped
 * by the inflight set.

packages/syntax-highlight/src/use-kgf-hi.name: colorForKind
packages/syntax-highlight/src/use-kgf-hi.doc:
* FNV-1a hash → deterministic HSL color for a token kind.
 *
 * Dark theme:  lightness 55-70%, saturation 55-80%  (bright on dark bg)
 * Light theme: lightness 25-40%, saturation 55-80%  (dark on light bg)
 *
 * Hue and saturation are identical across themes so the same token kind
 * keeps its colour identity; only lightness is flipped.

packages/vscode-plugin/vite.webview.conf.name: artifactPath
packages/vscode-plugin/vite.webview.conf.doc:
* When `moon build --target js` has been run, the MoonBit-compiled
 * tokenizer is available. Alias @indexion/kgf-tokenizer to it for
 * real syntax highlighting. Otherwise the package's default stub
 * export is used (returns empty tokens → plain text rendering).

packages/vscode-plugin/eslint/plugins/cu.name: collectExportPaths
packages/vscode-plugin/eslint/plugins/cu.doc: * Recursively collect all file-path strings from a package.json "exports" value.

packages/vscode-plugin/eslint/plugins/cu.name: toSourcePaths

packages/kgf-tokenizer/src/index.ts::val.name: warned
packages/kgf-tokenizer/src/index.ts::val.doc:
* @file KGF tokenizer — stub implementation.
 *
 * This is the default export when `moon build --target js` hasn't been run.
 * Returns an empty token array, causing syntax highlighting to fall through
 * to plain text rendering.
 *
 * When the MoonBit build artifact exists, consumer bundlers should alias
 * `@indexion/kgf-tokenizer` to the artifact path to get real tokenization:
 *
 *   _build/js/debug/build/cmd/kgf-tokenizer/kgf-tokenizer.js
 *
 * The MoonBit source lives at `cmd/kgf-tokenizer/main.mbt` and exports
 * the same `tokenize(spec_text, source) -> JSON string` signature.

src/pipeline/discover.mbt::value:Support.name: SupportedFile
src/pipeline/discover.mbt::value:Support.doc:
SoT: `path` is always a real filesystem path.
 Archive files are represented as a single entry with concatenated content.
 Virtual paths (archive-internal) never appear here — they are handled
 by `@common.collect_files` for per-inner-file expansion.

src/pipeline/discover.mbt::value:root_di.name: root_dir_from_paths
src/pipeline/discover.mbt::value:root_di.doc:
Derive root_dir from a list of target paths.
 Uses the first path, falling back to ".".
 SoT for root_dir derivation — all callers that have paths but not
 an explicit root_dir should use this instead of inline logic.

src/pipeline/discover.mbt::value:discove.name: discover_files
src/pipeline/discover.mbt::value:discove.doc:
Collect all file paths from given input paths.
 Supports glob patterns (e.g., "src/**/*.mbt").
 Respects includes, excludes, and recursion settings.
 Loads project-level ignore files from root_dir if specified.

src/pipeline/discover.mbt::value:build_f.name: build_filter_from_root
src/pipeline/discover.mbt::value:build_f.doc:
Build a FilterConfig with ignore patterns from root_dir only.
 Reads .gitignore and .indexionignore directly from root_dir without
 traversing up to git root — parent directories' ignore files are
 loaded incrementally during directory traversal in collect_files_impl.

src/pipeline/discover.mbt::value:should_.name: should_skip_dir
src/pipeline/discover.mbt::value:should_.doc:
Check if directory should be skipped (hidden, build artifacts, etc.).
 Uses cached default filter config for performance.

src/pipeline/discover.mbt::value:extract.name: extract_extension
src/pipeline/discover.mbt::value:extract.doc: Extract file extension from path (with dot).

src/pipeline/select.mbt::value:KGFIndex.name: KGFIndex
src/pipeline/select.mbt::value:KGFIndex.doc:
Index mapping source patterns to KGF file paths.
 Built once from headers, then used to select which KGFs to fully parse.

src/pipeline/select.mbt::value:build_kgf.name: build_kgf_index
src/pipeline/select.mbt::value:build_kgf.doc:
Build KGF index by reading only headers from all KGF files.
 This is fast because it only parses the header section.

src/pipeline/select.mbt::value:build_pat.name: build_path_signals
src/pipeline/select.mbt::value:build_pat.doc:
Build matching signals from file paths (extensions and basenames).
 These signals are used to select only relevant KGF specs.

src/pipeline/select.mbt::value:get_match.name: get_matching_kgf_paths
src/pipeline/select.mbt::value:get_match.doc: Get KGF file paths that match the given signals using the index.

src/pipeline/select.mbt::value:load_regi.name: load_registry_from_index
src/pipeline/select.mbt::value:load_regi.doc: Load registry from index, only parsing KGF files that match signals.

src/pipeline/select.mbt::value:sources_m.name: sources_match_signals
src/pipeline/select.mbt::value:sources_m.doc: Check if any source pattern matches the signals.

src/pipeline/select.mbt::value:load_regi.name: load_registry_for_paths
src/pipeline/select.mbt::value:load_regi.doc:
Load only relevant KGF specs from a directory based on discovered file paths.
 Optimized single-pass: reads each KGF file once, parses header, and if matched, full parses.

src/pipeline/comparison/candidates/candi.name: brute_force
src/pipeline/comparison/candidates/candi.doc: Generate all (i, j) pairs where i < j.

src/pipeline/comparison/candidates/candi.name: normalize_pairs
src/pipeline/comparison/candidates/candi.doc: Normalize raw TF-IDF results to (i, j) pairs where i < j.

src/pipeline/comparison/candidates/candi.name: pair_key
src/pipeline/comparison/candidates/candi.doc: Create a deduplication key for a pair (i, j).

src/pipeline/comparison/fdr.mbt::value:a.name: apply_fdr_correction
src/pipeline/comparison/fdr.mbt::value:a.doc:
Apply Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction to batch comparison results.

 `alpha` is the target FDR level (e.g., 0.05 for 5% expected false discoveries).
 `total_pairs` is the total number of pairs tested (N*(N-1)/2), needed
 to account for pairs that fell below the initial threshold.
 `threshold` is the similarity threshold that was used to produce the matches.

 Returns filtered matches that survive FDR correction.

src/pipeline/comparison/fdr.mbt::value:a.name: apply_fdr
src/pipeline/comparison/fdr.mbt::value:a.doc:
Apply FDR correction to a BatchResult, returning a new BatchResult
 with only statistically significant matches.

 `alpha` is the target FDR level.
 `threshold` is the similarity threshold used to generate the matches.
 If not provided, the minimum observed score is used.

src/pipeline/comparison/tree_extract/tre.name: content_to_simple_tree
src/pipeline/comparison/tree_extract/tre.doc: Build a simple tree from raw content (fallback when KGF unavailable).

src/pipeline/comparison/tfidf.mbt::value.name: compare_tfidf
src/pipeline/comparison/tfidf.mbt::value.doc: Run batch comparison using TF-IDF strategy.

src/pipeline/comparison/tfidf.mbt::value.name: compare_bm25
src/pipeline/comparison/tfidf.mbt::value.doc:
Run batch comparison using BM25 strategy.
 BM25 adds document length normalization and term frequency saturation
 over standard TF-IDF, producing more accurate similarity for documents
 of varying lengths.

src/pipeline/comparison/tfidf.mbt::value.name: compare_jsd
src/pipeline/comparison/tfidf.mbt::value.doc:
Run batch comparison using Jensen-Shannon Divergence.
 JSD measures divergence between token probability distributions,
 capturing different information than cosine similarity.

src/pipeline/comparison/types.mbt::value.name: FileEntry
src/pipeline/comparison/types.mbt::value.doc: A file entry with display name and content for batch comparison.

src/pipeline/comparison/types.mbt::value.name: SimilarityMatch
src/pipeline/comparison/types.mbt::value.doc: A similarity match between two files.

src/pipeline/comparison/types.mbt::value.name: create
src/pipeline/comparison/types.mbt::value.doc: Create a BatchConfig with custom values.

src/pipeline/comparison/types.mbt::value.name: compare
src/pipeline/comparison/types.mbt::value.doc: Run batch comparison with the specified strategy.

src/pipeline/comparison/types.mbt::value.name: filter_cross_directory
src/pipeline/comparison/types.mbt::value.doc: Filter matches to only cross-directory pairs.

src/pipeline/comparison/tree.mbt::value:.name: compare_apted
src/pipeline/comparison/tree.mbt::value:.doc: Run batch comparison using APTED strategy.

src/pipeline/comparison/tree.mbt::value:.name: compare_tsed
src/pipeline/comparison/tree.mbt::value:.doc: Run batch comparison using TSED strategy.

src/pipeline/comparison/tree.mbt::value:.name: compare_apted_functions
src/pipeline/comparison/tree.mbt::value:.doc:
Run function-level batch comparison using APTED strategy.
 Returns matches with qualified function names (file::func) in file1/file2.

src/pipeline/comparison/tree.mbt::value:.name: compare_tsed_functions
src/pipeline/comparison/tree.mbt::value:.doc:
Run function-level batch comparison using TSED strategy.
 Returns matches with qualified function names (file::func) in file1/file2.

src/pipeline/comparison/hybrid.mbt::valu.name: fuse_lexical
src/pipeline/comparison/hybrid.mbt::valu.doc:
Fuse BM25 and JSD into a single lexical evidence score.

 Rather than max or average, this considers the agreement between signals:
 - Both high and close together → strong evidence, slight bonus
 - One high and one low → weaker evidence, discounted
 - Both low → low score (correctly reflects low similarity)

src/pipeline/comparison/hybrid.mbt::valu.name: fuse_final
src/pipeline/comparison/hybrid.mbt::valu.doc:
Fuse lexical evidence with structural evidence into final score.

 The key insight: structural similarity is the stronger signal for
 code duplication (catches renamed copies), but lexical provides
 context that prevents structural false positives (e.g., two simple
 for-loops that happen to have the same structure).

 - Both high → high confidence, slight boost
 - Lexical high, structural low → structural contradicts: discount
 - Structural high, lexical low → possible renamed copy, but uncertain
 - Only lexical available → pass through (no structural evidence to use)

src/pipeline/comparison/hybrid.mbt::valu.name: compare_hybrid
src/pipeline/comparison/hybrid.mbt::valu.doc: Hybrid comparison with multi-signal evidence fusion.

src/pipeline/comparison/hybrid.mbt::valu.name: select_structural_mode
src/pipeline/comparison/hybrid.mbt::valu.doc:
Structural mode selection:
 0 = lexical only (too many files or trees too large)
 1 = brute-force TSED (small dataset, every pair)
 2 = prefiltered TSED (medium dataset, BM25 candidates only)
 3 = strict prefiltered TSED (large dataset, tighter BM25 filter)

src/pipeline/comparison/hybrid.mbt::valu.name: emit_lexical_only
src/pipeline/comparison/hybrid.mbt::valu.doc: Emit results when only lexical evidence is available.

src/pipeline/vocabulary/vocabulary.mbt::.name: GapTerm
src/pipeline/vocabulary/vocabulary.mbt::.doc: A term found in source but absent from target.

src/pipeline/vocabulary/vocabulary.mbt::.name: measure_divergence

Measure vocabulary divergence between two sets of per-file token arrays.

 Builds project-wide document frequency from all files for ubiquity filtering,
 then extracts gap terms using module-local TF-IDF with Kneedle thresholding.

src/kgf/cas/types/types.mbt::value:Modul.name: ModuleChunk
src/kgf/cas/types/types.mbt::value:Modul.doc:
A chunk represents a single module's contribution to the graph.
 Each source file produces one chunk containing:

src/kgf/cas/types/types.mbt::value:CASMa.name: CASManifest
src/kgf/cas/types/types.mbt::value:CASMa.doc:
Root manifest for a KGF CAS store.

src/kgf/cas/assembler/assembler.mbt::val.name: all_resolved

src/kgf/cas/hash/hash.mbt::value:compute.name: compute_hash
src/kgf/cas/hash/hash.mbt::value:compute.doc: Compute SHA-256 hash of content, truncated to 32 hex characters.

src/kgf/cas/hash/hash.mbt::value:compute.name: compute_spec_hash

src/kgf/cas/manifest/manifest.mbt::value.doc:
Get current timestamp as ISO 8601 string.

src/kgf/cas/store/store.mbt::value:Chunk.name: ChunkStore
src/kgf/cas/store/store.mbt::value:Chunk.doc: Chunk store trait for reading/writing module chunks.

src/kgf/cas/store/store.mbt::value:Memor.name: MemoryChunkStore
src/kgf/cas/store/store.mbt::value:Memor.doc:
In-memory chunk store implementation.
 Useful for testing and short-lived sessions where persistence isn't needed.

src/kgf/cas/store/store.mbt::value:new.name: new
src/kgf/cas/store/store.mbt::value:new.doc: Create a new in-memory chunk store.

src/kgf/cas/store/store.mbt::value:size.name: size
src/kgf/cas/store/store.mbt::value:size.doc: Get the number of chunks in the store.

src/kgf/cas/store/store.mbt::value:clear.name: clear
src/kgf/cas/store/store.mbt::value:clear.doc: Clear all chunks from the store.

src/kgf/types/classify.mbt::value:Classi.name: ClassifyModel
src/kgf/types/classify.mbt::value:Classi.doc:
ClassifyModel represents a trained classification model persisted in
 a KGF `=== classify` section.

 This is NOT a hand-authored vocabulary list. The weights are computed
 from a document corpus by a training process (e.g., `indexion classify train`)
 and serialized into the KGF spec for later inference.

 ## Model structure

 A model consists of:
 - **Metadata**: model type, training parameters, corpus statistics
 - **Classes**: named classification categories, each with:
   - A prior log-probability (base rate from training corpus)
   - A vocabulary of terms with conditional log-probabilities P(term|class)

 ## Inference

 Given a text to classify, a consumer:
 1. Tokenizes the text into terms
 2. For each class, sums the prior + Σ log P(term|class) for observed terms
 3. The class with the highest posterior score wins

 The model is agnostic to what the classes represent — normative vs. meta,
 requirement vs. boilerplate, etc. Class semantics are determined by the
 KGF spec author and the training corpus.

src/kgf/types/classify.mbt::value:Classi.name: ClassifyClass
src/kgf/types/classify.mbt::value:Classi.doc: A single classification class with its prior and vocabulary.

src/kgf/types/classify.mbt::value:get_me.name: get_meta
src/kgf/types/classify.mbt::value:get_me.doc: Returns a metadata value by key, or None.

src/kgf/types/classify.mbt::value:get_cl.name: get_class
src/kgf/types/classify.mbt::value:get_cl.doc: Returns a class definition by name.

src/kgf/types/classify.mbt::value:is_emp.name: is_empty
src/kgf/types/classify.mbt::value:is_emp.doc: Returns true if this model has no classes.

src/kgf/types/classify.mbt::value:classi.name: classify
src/kgf/types/classify.mbt::value:classi.doc:
Classify a tokenized text by computing posterior scores for each class.
 Returns an array of (class_name, score) pairs sorted by descending score.

 The score for each class is: prior + Σ vocab[term] for observed terms.
 Terms not in the class vocabulary contribute 0 (equivalent to uniform
 smoothing at the training-time default).

src/kgf/types/classify.mbt::value:predic.name: predict
src/kgf/types/classify.mbt::value:predic.doc:
Returns the predicted class name (highest posterior score).
 Returns None if the model has no classes.

src/kgf/types/classify.mbt::value:term_w.name: term_weight
src/kgf/types/classify.mbt::value:term_w.doc:
Returns the conditional log-probability of a term in this class.
 Returns 0.0 for unknown terms (smoothed default).

src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:KGFSpec.name: KGFSpec
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:KGFSpec.doc:
KGFSpec represents the complete KGF specification for a language.

 Sections are parsed lazily: the raw text for each === section is stored
 at construction time and the heavy parsing (lex patterns, grammar rules,
 semantic blocks, etc.) is deferred until the first accessor call.
 Once parsed, results are memoized in the corresponding cache field.

 Phase 1 (eager, at registry load):
   language, resolver, features, ignore
 Phase 2 (lazy, on first access):
   tokens, keyword_kinds        — lex section (pattern compilation is heavy)
   rules, attrs                 — grammar + attrs sections
   semantics                    — semantics section
   preprocess, preprocess_fst   — preprocess sections

src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:new_lazy.name: new_lazy
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:new_lazy.doc:
Creates a KGFSpec with deferred section parsing.
 Only language, resolver, features, and ignore are parsed eagerly.
 The raw section texts are stored for lazy parsing on first access.

src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_tokens.name: get_tokens
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_tokens.doc: Returns the tokens (lazy: parses lex section on first call).

src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_keywor.name: get_keyword_kinds
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_keywor.doc: Returns the keyword kinds set (lazy: parses lex section on first call).

src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:is_keyword.name: is_keyword
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:is_keyword.doc: Returns true if the given token kind is classified as a keyword.

src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_rules.name: get_rules
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_rules.doc: Returns the rules (lazy: parses grammar section on first call).

src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_rule.name: get_rule
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_rule.doc: Returns a specific rule by name.

src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_attrs.name: get_attrs
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_attrs.doc: Returns the attributes (lazy: parses attrs section on first call).

src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_attr.name: get_attr
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_attr.doc: Returns a specific attribute actions by name.

src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_resolv.name: get_resolver
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_resolv.doc: Returns the resolver configuration (eager — always available).

src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_featur.name: get_features
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_featur.doc: Returns KGF feature metadata (eager — always available).

src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_featur.name: get_feature
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_featur.doc: Returns a specific feature value list by name.

src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_semant.name: get_semantics
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_semant.doc: Returns the semantics (lazy: parses semantics section on first call).

src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_semant.name: get_semantic
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_semant.doc: Returns specific semantic blocks by rule name.

src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_classi.name: get_classify
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_classi.doc:
Returns the classify model (lazy: parses classify section on first call).
 The model contains trained weights computed from a document corpus,
 not hand-authored vocabulary lists.

src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_ignore.name: get_ignore
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_ignore.doc: Returns the ignore patterns for this language (eager — always available).

src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_start_.name: get_start_rule
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:get_start_.doc:
Returns the start rule name.
 Uses convention: "Module" > "Program" > "Doc" > "File" > first rule found.

src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:ensure_lex.name: ensure_lex_parsed
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:ensure_lex.doc:
Parse the lex section if not yet parsed, and cache the results.
 Uses the `parse_lex_section` callback registered by the parser package.

src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:ensure_gra.name: ensure_grammar_parsed
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:ensure_gra.doc: Parse the grammar section if not yet parsed.

src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:ensure_att.name: ensure_attrs_parsed
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:ensure_att.doc: Parse the attrs section if not yet parsed.

src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:ensure_sem.name: ensure_semantics_parsed
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:ensure_sem.doc: Parse the semantics section if not yet parsed.

src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:ensure_pre.name: ensure_preprocess_parsed
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:ensure_pre.doc: Parse the preprocess section if not yet parsed.

src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:ensure_pre.name: ensure_preprocess_fst_parsed
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:ensure_pre.doc: Parse the preprocess_fst section if not yet parsed.

src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:ensure_cla.name: ensure_classify_parsed
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::value:ensure_cla.doc: Parse the classify section if not yet parsed.

src/kgf/types/preprocess.mbt::value:FstA.name: FstAction
src/kgf/types/preprocess.mbt::value:FstA.doc: FstAction represents an action to perform during FST state transition.

src/kgf/types/preprocess.mbt::value:emit.name: emit
src/kgf/types/preprocess.mbt::value:emit.doc: Creates an Emit action with the specified value.

src/kgf/types/preprocess.mbt::value:flus.name: flush
src/kgf/types/preprocess.mbt::value:flus.doc: Creates a Flush action with the specified padding.

src/kgf/types/preprocess.mbt::value:FstT.name: FstTransition
src/kgf/types/preprocess.mbt::value:FstT.doc: FstTransition represents a single state transition in the FST.

src/kgf/types/preprocess.mbt::value:get_.name: get_on
src/kgf/types/preprocess.mbt::value:get_.doc: Returns the input class to match.

src/kgf/types/preprocess.mbt::value:Prep.name: PreprocessFstSpec
src/kgf/types/preprocess.mbt::value:Prep.doc: PreprocessFstSpec represents a JSON-defined preprocess FST specification.

src/kgf/types/rule.mbt::value:RuleDef.name: RuleDef
src/kgf/types/rule.mbt::value:RuleDef.doc:
RuleDef represents a grammar rule definition.
 Contains the rule name and its expression.

src/kgf/types/rule.mbt::value:new.name: new
src/kgf/types/rule.mbt::value:new.doc: Creates a new RuleDef with the specified parameters.

src/kgf/types/rule.mbt::value:get_name.name: get_name
src/kgf/types/rule.mbt::value:get_name.doc: Returns the name of the rule.

src/kgf/types/rule.mbt::value:get_expr.name: get_expr
src/kgf/types/rule.mbt::value:get_expr.doc: Returns the expression of the rule.

src/kgf/types/rule.mbt::value:AttrAction.name: AttrAction
src/kgf/types/rule.mbt::value:AttrAction.doc:
AttrAction represents an attribute action in the grammar.
 Kind can be: "def", "ref", "call", "argref", "import", "reexport",
 "scope", "arg_begin", "arg_end", "call_end", etc.

src/kgf/types/rule.mbt::value:get_kind.name: get_kind
src/kgf/types/rule.mbt::value:get_kind.doc: Returns the kind of the attribute action.

src/kgf/types/rule.mbt::value:get_params.name: get_params
src/kgf/types/rule.mbt::value:get_params.doc: Returns the params of the attribute action.

src/kgf/types/rule.mbt::value:get_param.name: get_param
src/kgf/types/rule.mbt::value:get_param.doc: Returns a specific parameter value by key.

src/kgf/types/token.mbt::value:TokenDef.name: TokenDef
src/kgf/types/token.mbt::value:TokenDef.doc:
TokenDef represents a token definition in the KGF grammar.
 Contains the token name, regex pattern, skip flag, and optional flags.

src/kgf/types/token.mbt::value:get_name.name: get_name
src/kgf/types/token.mbt::value:get_name.doc: Returns the name of the token.

src/kgf/types/token.mbt::value:get_patte.name: get_pattern
src/kgf/types/token.mbt::value:get_patte.doc: Returns the pattern of the token.

src/kgf/types/token.mbt::value:is_skip.name: is_skip
src/kgf/types/token.mbt::value:is_skip.doc: Returns whether this token should be skipped.

src/kgf/types/token.mbt::value:get_flags.name: get_flags
src/kgf/types/token.mbt::value:get_flags.doc: Returns the flags of the token if any.

src/kgf/types/token.mbt::value:is_skip_v.name: is_skip_value
src/kgf/types/token.mbt::value:is_skip_v.doc: Returns whether this token's value should be empty.

src/kgf/types/token.mbt::value:Tok.name: Tok
src/kgf/types/token.mbt::value:Tok.doc: Tok represents a lexed token with position information.

src/kgf/types/token.mbt::value:new.name: new
src/kgf/types/token.mbt::value:new.doc: Creates a new Tok with the specified parameters.

src/kgf/types/token.mbt::value:get_kind.name: get_kind
src/kgf/types/token.mbt::value:get_kind.doc: Returns the kind of the token.

src/kgf/types/token.mbt::value:get_text.name: get_text
src/kgf/types/token.mbt::value:get_text.doc: Returns the text of the token.

src/kgf/types/token.mbt::value:get_value.name: get_value
src/kgf/types/token.mbt::value:get_value.doc:
Returns the value of the token (first capture group when present).
 Defaults to text if not set.

src/kgf/types/token.mbt::value:get_pos.name: get_pos
src/kgf/types/token.mbt::value:get_pos.doc: Returns the position of the token in the source.

src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:Module.name: ModulePathStyle
src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:Module.doc: ModulePathStyle represents the style of module path separators.

src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:Resolv.name: ResolveStepType
src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:Resolv.doc: ResolveStepType represents the type of resolution step.

src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:Resolv.name: ResolveStep
src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:Resolv.doc: ResolveStep represents a single step in the resolution chain.

src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:exact.name: exact
src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:exact.doc: Creates an Exact resolve step (checks target path as-is).

src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:ancest.name: ancestors
src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:ancest.doc: Creates an Ancestors resolve step (walks up from file to root).

src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:progre.name: progressive
src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:progre.doc:
Creates a Progressive resolve step — the chain driver retries the
 preceding file-probe steps (exact/index/ext/sibling) with successively
 shorter path suffixes stripped. Needed for languages where an import
 path can address an item nested inside a module file.

src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:has_co.name: has_condition
src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:has_co.doc: Checks if this step has a condition.

src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:Resolv.name: ResolverAlias
src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:Resolv.doc: ResolverAlias represents a path alias for module resolution.

src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:new.name: new
src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:new.doc: Creates a new ResolverAlias with the specified parameters.

src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:get_pa.name: get_pattern
src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:get_pa.doc: Returns the pattern of the alias.

src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:get_re.name: get_replace
src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:get_re.doc: Returns the replacement of the alias.

src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:Resolv.name: ResolverSpec
src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:Resolv.doc: ResolverSpec represents the configuration for module resolution.

src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:has_re.name: has_resolve_chain
src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:has_re.doc: Checks if the resolver has a resolve chain defined.

src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:get_pr.name: get_project_config
src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:get_pr.doc: Returns the project config file name (e.g., moon.mod.json).

src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:Resolu.name: ResolutionStats
src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:Resolu.doc: ResolutionStats tracks statistics for module resolution performance.

src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:Resolu.name: ResolutionCache
src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:Resolu.doc: ResolutionCache caches module resolution results for performance.

src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:make_k.name: make_key
src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:make_k.doc: Generates a cache key from from_file and module_path.

src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:is_res.name: is_resolving
src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:is_res.doc: Checks if a module is currently being resolved (circular detection).

src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:push_r.name: push_resolving
src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:push_r.doc: Pushes a module onto the resolution stack.

src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:pop_re.name: pop_resolving
src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:pop_re.doc: Pops a module from the resolution stack.

src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:circul.name: circular_sentinel_prefix
src/kgf/types/resolver.mbt::value:circul.doc: Sentinel prefix for circular dependency detection.

src/kgf/types/semantics.mbt::value:SemEx.name: SemExpr
src/kgf/types/semantics.mbt::value:SemEx.doc: SemExpr represents a semantic expression in the KGF DSL.

src/kgf/types/semantics.mbt::value:SemSt.name: SemStmt
src/kgf/types/semantics.mbt::value:SemSt.doc: SemStmt represents a semantic statement in the KGF DSL.

src/kgf/types/semantics.mbt::value:SemOn.name: SemOnBlock
src/kgf/types/semantics.mbt::value:SemOn.doc:
SemOnBlock represents a semantic on-block definition.
 Contains a rule name, optional when condition, then statements, and optional else statements.

src/kgf/classify/train.mbt::value:Traini.name: TrainingSample
src/kgf/classify/train.mbt::value:Traini.doc: Training corpus entry: a single labeled section.

src/kgf/classify/train.mbt::value:fit_na.name: fit_naive_bayes
src/kgf/classify/train.mbt::value:fit_na.doc:
Fit a Naive Bayes model from labeled samples.

 Uses Laplace smoothing (add-1) to handle unseen terms.
 All weights are stored as log-probabilities.

src/kgf/classify/train.mbt::value:tokeni.name: tokenize_for_training
src/kgf/classify/train.mbt::value:tokeni.doc:
Tokenize text into lowercase words for training.
 Extracts words of 2+ characters, lowercased.

src/kgf/classify/train.mbt::value:format.name: format_double
src/kgf/classify/train.mbt::value:format.doc: Format a Double with fixed decimal places.

src/kgf/classify/train.mbt::value:json_i.name: json_int
src/kgf/classify/train.mbt::value:json_i.doc: Extract an integer from a JSON payload.

src/kgf/classify/train.mbt::value:json_s.name: json_str
src/kgf/classify/train.mbt::value:json_s.doc: Extract a string from a JSON payload.

src/kgf/lexer/lexer.mbt::value:Lexer.name: Lexer
src/kgf/lexer/lexer.mbt::value:Lexer.doc: Lexer transforms input text into tokens based on token definitions.

src/kgf/lexer/lexer.mbt::value:CompiledT.name: CompiledToken
src/kgf/lexer/lexer.mbt::value:CompiledT.doc: Compiled token matcher used by the lexer hot path.

src/kgf/lexer/lexer.mbt::value:tokenize.name: tokenize
src/kgf/lexer/lexer.mbt::value:tokenize.doc:
Tokenizes the input string and returns an array of tokens.
 Skipped tokens (like whitespace) are not included in the result.

src/kgf/lexer/lexer.mbt::value:try_match.name: try_match
src/kgf/lexer/lexer.mbt::value:try_match.doc:
Tries to match a token at the given position.
 Returns the matched token definition, end position, matched text, and first capture group.

src/kgf/lexer/lexer.mbt::value:has_case_.name: has_case_insensitive_flag
src/kgf/lexer/lexer.mbt::value:has_case_.doc: Returns true when regex flags include case-insensitive matching.

src/kgf/lexer/lexer.mbt::value:extract_q.name: extract_quoted_value
src/kgf/lexer/lexer.mbt::value:extract_q.doc:
Extracts the value from a token text.
 For quoted strings, strips the surrounding quotes.

src/kgf/lexer/lexer.mbt::value:lexer.name: lexer
src/kgf/lexer/lexer.mbt::value:lexer.doc: Creates a lexer builder for fluent API usage.

src/kgf/lexer/lexer.mbt::value:LexerBuil.name: LexerBuilder
src/kgf/lexer/lexer.mbt::value:LexerBuil.doc: Builder for constructing a Lexer with token definitions.

src/kgf/lexer/lexer.mbt::value:build.name: build
src/kgf/lexer/lexer.mbt::value:build.doc: Builds the lexer from the accumulated token definitions.

src/kgf/lexer/diagnostics.mbt::value:Lex.name: LexDiagnostic
src/kgf/lexer/diagnostics.mbt::value:Lex.doc:
LexDiagnostic describes a span of source text that did not match any lexer
 token rule.

src/kgf/lexer/diagnostics.mbt::value:new.name: new
src/kgf/lexer/diagnostics.mbt::value:new.doc: Creates a lexical diagnostic for an unmatched source span.

src/kgf/lexer/diagnostics.mbt::value:tok.name: tokenize_with_diagnostics
src/kgf/lexer/diagnostics.mbt::value:tok.doc: Tokenizes input and returns lexer diagnostics.

src/kgf/features/extract.mbt::value:Extr.name: ExtractedFunction
src/kgf/features/extract.mbt::value:Extr.doc: Extracted function with its token range.

src/kgf/features/features.mbt::value:is_.name: is_identifier_kind
src/kgf/features/features.mbt::value:is_.doc:
Check if token kind represents an identifier, using a prebuilt identifier set.
 The set should be obtained from `build_identifier_set`.

src/kgf/features/features.mbt::value:nod.name: node_type_code
src/kgf/features/features.mbt::value:nod.doc: Node type label for code-oriented files (content_type absent or non-document).

src/kgf/features/features.mbt::value:nod.name: node_type_document
src/kgf/features/features.mbt::value:nod.doc: Node type label for document-oriented files (content_type: document).

src/kgf/features/proxy.mbt::value:ProxyF.name: ProxyFunction
src/kgf/features/proxy.mbt::value:ProxyF.doc: A detected proxy function that delegates to another function.

src/kgf/features/proxy.mbt::value:Unwrap.name: UnwrapEdit
src/kgf/features/proxy.mbt::value:Unwrap.doc: A text edit for unwrapping a call site.

src/kgf/features/proxy.mbt::value:is_ski.name: is_skip_token
src/kgf/features/proxy.mbt::value:is_ski.doc:
Token kinds to skip when analyzing function body.
 Skips whitespace/newlines (NL, SKIP, WS), comments (*Comment*),
 and doc tokens (Doc* prefix). These are KGF naming conventions
 shared across all language specs.

src/kgf/features/proxy.mbt::value:CallTa.name: CallTargetResult
src/kgf/features/proxy.mbt::value:CallTa.doc:
Result of building a call target from tokens.
 Carries the target string along with metadata about its structure,
 so callers don't need to re-parse the string with hardcoded patterns.

src/kgf/features/proxy.mbt::value:check_.name: check_all_forwarded
src/kgf/features/proxy.mbt::value:check_.doc: Check if all function parameters are forwarded in the call arguments.

src/kgf/features/proxy.mbt::value:apply_.name: apply_edits
src/kgf/features/proxy.mbt::value:apply_.doc:
Apply character-offset edits to content string.
 Sorts edits by offset descending (back-to-front) so earlier offsets stay valid.

src/kgf/features/proxy.mbt::value:delete.name: delete_lines
src/kgf/features/proxy.mbt::value:delete.doc:
Delete lines from content (1-indexed, inclusive range).
 Also removes a blank line left after deletion if present.

src/kgf/preprocess/preprocess.mbt::value.name: base64_to_hex
src/kgf/preprocess/preprocess.mbt::value.doc: Converts a base64 string to hexadecimal.

src/kgf/preprocess/preprocess.mbt::value.name: is_base64_char
src/kgf/preprocess/preprocess.mbt::value.doc: Checks if a character is a valid base64 character.

src/kgf/preprocess/preprocess.mbt::value.name: decode_base64
src/kgf/preprocess/preprocess.mbt::value.doc: Decodes a base64 string to bytes.

src/kgf/preprocess/preprocess.mbt::value.name: base64_value
src/kgf/preprocess/preprocess.mbt::value.doc: Gets the numeric value of a base64 character.

src/kgf/preprocess/preprocess.mbt::value.name: byte_to_hex
src/kgf/preprocess/preprocess.mbt::value.doc: Converts a byte to uppercase hexadecimal string.

src/kgf/preprocess/fst.mbt::value:Compil.name: CompiledCharClass
src/kgf/preprocess/fst.mbt::value:Compil.doc: CompiledCharClass represents a compiled character class for matching.

src/kgf/preprocess/fst.mbt::value:Compil.name: CompiledFst
src/kgf/preprocess/fst.mbt::value:Compil.doc: CompiledFst represents a compiled FST ready for execution.

src/kgf/preprocess/fst.mbt::value:compil.name: compile_char_class
src/kgf/preprocess/fst.mbt::value:compil.doc:
Compiles a character class pattern into a CompiledCharClass.
 Supports patterns like: [A-Za-z0-9+/=]

src/kgf/preprocess/fst.mbt::value:char_m.name: char_matches_class
src/kgf/preprocess/fst.mbt::value:char_m.doc: Checks if a character matches a compiled character class.

src/kgf/preprocess/fst.mbt::value:classi.name: classify_char
src/kgf/preprocess/fst.mbt::value:classi.doc:
Classifies a character according to the FST's character classes.
 Returns the name of the matching class, or None if no match.

src/kgf/preprocess/fst.mbt::value:sextet.name: sextet
src/kgf/preprocess/fst.mbt::value:sextet.doc: Converts a base64 character to its sextet value.

src/kgf/preprocess/fst.mbt::value:decode.name: decode_quartet
src/kgf/preprocess/fst.mbt::value:decode.doc: Decodes a quartet of base64 sextets into bytes.

src/kgf/preprocess/fst.mbt::value:is_ws_.name: is_ws_byte
src/kgf/preprocess/fst.mbt::value:is_ws_.doc: Checks if a byte is whitespace.

src/kgf/preprocess/fst.mbt::value:is_ctr.name: is_ctrl_byte
src/kgf/preprocess/fst.mbt::value:is_ctr.doc: Checks if a byte is a control character.

src/kgf/preprocess/fst.mbt::value:classi.name: classify_utf8
src/kgf/preprocess/fst.mbt::value:classi.doc: Classifies a byte array as UTF-8 text.

src/kgf/preprocess/fst.mbt::value:run_fs.name: run_fst
src/kgf/preprocess/fst.mbt::value:run_fs.doc: Runs a compiled FST over the input string and returns the transformed output.

src/kgf/semantics/eval_attrs.mbt::value:.name: resolve_import_path
src/kgf/semantics/eval_attrs.mbt::value:.doc: Resolves an import path to a module ID.

src/kgf/semantics/eval_attrs.mbt::value:.name: is_relative_path
src/kgf/semantics/eval_attrs.mbt::value:.doc: Checks if a path is relative (starts with ./ or ../ or /).

src/kgf/semantics/eval_attrs.mbt::value:.name: eval_rule_attrs
src/kgf/semantics/eval_attrs.mbt::value:.doc:
Evaluates attrs for a rule by name.
 Looks up the attrs for the rule in the spec and evaluates them.

src/kgf/semantics/eval_semantics.mbt::va.name: eval_rule_semantics
src/kgf/semantics/eval_semantics.mbt::va.doc:
Evaluates semantics for a rule by name.
 First processes attrs (simple annotations), then semantics DSL blocks.

src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:i.name: is_truthy
src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:i.doc:
Checks if a value is truthy according to KGF semantics.
 - null/undefined -> false
 - boolean -> itself
 - empty string -> false
 - empty array -> false
 - other -> true

src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:j.name: json_to_string
src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:j.doc: Converts a Json value to a string.

src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:j.name: json_to_number
src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:j.doc: Converts a Json value to a number.

src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:e.name: eval_func_obj
src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:e.doc:
Evaluates the obj() function to create an object from key-value pairs.
 Null values are skipped — obj("a", "1", "b", null) produces {"a": "1"}.

src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:t.name: trim_string
src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:t.doc: Trims leading and trailing whitespace from a string.

src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:i.name: is_whitespace
src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:i.doc: Checks if a character is whitespace.

src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:i.name: is_valid_hex
src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:i.doc: Checks if a string is a valid hexadecimal number.

src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:i.name: is_hex_char
src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:i.doc: Checks if a character is a valid hex digit.

src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:p.name: parse_hex
src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:p.doc: Parses a hexadecimal string to an integer.

src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:h.name: hex_char_value
src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:h.doc: Gets the numeric value of a hex character.

src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:e.name: eval_func_to_upper
src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:e.doc:
Evaluates the toUpper() function.
 Converts ASCII letters in the string to uppercase.

src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:e.name: eval_func_to_lower
src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:e.doc:
Evaluates the toLower() function.
 Converts ASCII letters in the string to lowercase.

src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:e.name: eval_func_cond
src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:e.doc:
Evaluates the cond() function — conditional expression.
 cond(condition, then_value) returns then_value if condition is truthy, null otherwise.
 cond(condition, then_value, else_value) returns then_value or else_value.
 Combined with obj()'s null-skipping, enables conditional attributes
 without when/else duplication in KGF specs.

src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:i.name: is_known_call
src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:i.doc:
Returns true if `name` is a known context-dependent call ($resolve, $scope, etc.).
 SoT: the match arms in eval_call above.

src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:i.name: is_known_func
src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:i.doc:
Returns true if `name` is a known pure built-in function (concat, obj, etc.).
 SoT: the match arms in eval_func above.

src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:i.name: is_known_builtin_var
src/kgf/semantics/eval_expr.mbt::value:i.doc:
Returns true if `name` is a known built-in variable ($file, $root, etc.).
 SoT: the if-chain in eval_var above.

src/kgf/semantics/context.mbt::value:Sco.name: ScopeEntry
src/kgf/semantics/context.mbt::value:Sco.doc: ScopeEntry represents a single scope frame with value and type namespaces.

src/kgf/semantics/context.mbt::value:get.name: get_value

src/kgf/semantics/context.mbt::value:get.name: get_type

src/kgf/semantics/context.mbt::value:set.name: set_value

src/kgf/semantics/context.mbt::value:set.name: set_type

src/kgf/semantics/context.mbt::value:Env.name: Env

SemEvalCtx represents the evaluation context for semantics processing.

src/kgf/semantics/context.mbt::value:pop.name: pop_scope

src/kgf/semantics/context.mbt::value:loo.name: lookup_scope
src/kgf/semantics/context.mbt::value:loo.doc: Looks up a name in the scope stack (searches from top to bottom).

src/kgf/semantics/context.mbt::value:cur.name: current_call_id
src/kgf/semantics/context.mbt::value:cur.doc: Gets the current call ID from the call stack.

src/kgf/semantics/context.mbt::value:nex.name: next_sym_id
src/kgf/semantics/context.mbt::value:nex.doc: Generates the next auto symbol ID.

src/kgf/semantics/context.mbt::value:nex.name: next_call_id
src/kgf/semantics/context.mbt::value:nex.doc: Generates the next auto call ID.

src/kgf/semantics/context.mbt::value:add.name: add_event

src/kgf/semantics/context.mbt::value:col.name: collect_refs

src/kgf/declarations/declarations.mbt::v.name: SpanPreference
src/kgf/declarations/declarations.mbt::v.doc: Span selection preference for declaration matching.

src/kgf/parser/parse_resolver.mbt::value.doc:
Parses resolver block into normalized ResolverSpec.

src/kgf/parser/parse_resolver.mbt::value.doc:
Parses resolver block with sources from header.
 Header sources take precedence over resolver section sources.

src/kgf/parser/parse_resolver.mbt::value.doc: Checks if a line is indented (starts with space or tab).

src/kgf/parser/parse_resolver.mbt::value.doc: Parses a multi-line resolve step (indented key-value pairs).

src/kgf/parser/parse_resolver.mbt::value.name: get_string
src/kgf/parser/parse_resolver.mbt::value.doc: Gets a string value from the key-value map.

src/kgf/parser/parse_resolver.mbt::value.name: get_optional_string
src/kgf/parser/parse_resolver.mbt::value.doc: Gets an optional string value from the key-value map.

src/kgf/parser/parse_resolver.mbt::value.name: get_int
src/kgf/parser/parse_resolver.mbt::value.doc: Gets an integer value from the key-value map.

src/kgf/parser/parse_resolver.mbt::value.name: parse_int_str
src/kgf/parser/parse_resolver.mbt::value.doc: Parses a string to an integer.

src/kgf/parser/parse_lex.mbt::value:LexR.name: LexResult
src/kgf/parser/parse_lex.mbt::value:LexR.doc:
Result of parsing a lex section.
 Contains the token definitions and the set of token names declared as keywords.

src/kgf/parser/parse_lex.mbt::value:pars.name: parse_lex
src/kgf/parser/parse_lex.mbt::value:pars.doc:
Parses the lex section into token definitions and keyword classification.

 Syntax:
   SKIP /pattern/
   TOKEN NAME /pattern/[flags]
   KEYWORD NAME /pattern/[flags]

src/kgf/parser/parse_lex.mbt::value:pars.name: parse_named_token_rule
src/kgf/parser/parse_lex.mbt::value:pars.doc:
Parses a named token rule (TOKEN or KEYWORD): PREFIX NAME /pattern/[flags] [skip_value]
 `prefix_len` is the length of the leading keyword ("TOKEN" = 5, "KEYWORD" = 7).

src/kgf/parser/parse_lex.mbt::value:pars.name: parse_token_attrs
src/kgf/parser/parse_lex.mbt::value:pars.doc: Parses token attributes after the pattern: flags, skip_value, category.

src/kgf/parser/parse_lex.mbt::value:find.name: find_closing_slash
src/kgf/parser/parse_lex.mbt::value:find.doc: Finds the closing slash, handling escaped characters.

src/kgf/parser/parse_lex.mbt::value:read.name: read_identifier
src/kgf/parser/parse_lex.mbt::value:read.doc: Reads an identifier from the text.

src/kgf/parser/parse_lex.mbt::value:is_v.name: is_valid_flags
src/kgf/parser/parse_lex.mbt::value:is_v.doc: Checks if a string contains only valid regex flags.

src/kgf/parser/parse_preprocess_fst.mbt:.name: parse_preprocess_fst
src/kgf/parser/parse_preprocess_fst.mbt:.doc:
Parses preprocess_fst section as JSON (single object or array).
 Returns None if the section is empty.

src/kgf/parser/parse_preprocess_fst.mbt:.name: parse_single_fst_spec
src/kgf/parser/parse_preprocess_fst.mbt:.doc: Parses a single FST spec from JSON.

src/kgf/parser/parse_preprocess_fst.mbt:.name: parse_fst_transition
src/kgf/parser/parse_preprocess_fst.mbt:.doc: Parses a single FST transition from JSON.

src/kgf/parser/parse_preprocess_fst.mbt:.name: parse_fst_action
src/kgf/parser/parse_preprocess_fst.mbt:.doc: Parses a single FST action from JSON.

src/kgf/parser/parse_attrs.mbt::value:pa.name: parse_attrs
src/kgf/parser/parse_attrs.mbt::value:pa.doc:
Parses simple one-line attribute actions per rule into structured form.

 Syntax:
   on RuleName: kind1 key1=val1; kind2; ...

src/kgf/parser/parse_attrs.mbt::value:pa.name: parse_attr_line
src/kgf/parser/parse_attrs.mbt::value:pa.doc: Parses a single attribute line: on RuleName: kind1 key1=val1; kind2; ...

src/kgf/parser/parse_attrs.mbt::value:pa.name: parse_action_list
src/kgf/parser/parse_attrs.mbt::value:pa.doc: Parses a list of actions separated by semicolons.

src/kgf/parser/parse_attrs.mbt::value:pa.name: parse_single_action
src/kgf/parser/parse_attrs.mbt::value:pa.doc:
Parses a single action: kind [implicit_id] key1=val1 key2=val2 ...
 For example: def id kind=Variable ns=value
   - kind = "def"
   - implicit "id" param (first non-key=value token) = "id" (label name)
   - kind = "Variable"
   - ns = "value"

src/kgf/parser/parse_kgf.mbt::value:sect.name: section
src/kgf/parser/parse_kgf.mbt::value:sect.doc:
Extracts a section body by heading marker `=== name`.
 Uses line-start matching to avoid false positives from `===` inside patterns.

src/kgf/parser/parse_kgf.mbt::value:extr.name: extract_header
src/kgf/parser/parse_kgf.mbt::value:extr.doc:
Extracts the header section (everything before the first `===`).
 Uses line-start matching to avoid false positives from `===` inside patterns.

src/kgf/parser/parse_kgf.mbt::value:extr.name: extract_language
src/kgf/parser/parse_kgf.mbt::value:extr.doc: Extracts language name from the header.

src/kgf/parser/parse_kgf.mbt::value:extr.name: extract_sources
src/kgf/parser/parse_kgf.mbt::value:extr.doc:
Extracts sources from the header.
 Returns array of source patterns (e.g., [".mbt", ".ts"]).

src/kgf/parser/parse_kgf.mbt::value:pars.name: parse_comma_list
src/kgf/parser/parse_kgf.mbt::value:pars.doc: Parses a comma-separated list of values.

src/kgf/parser/parse_kgf.mbt::value:KGFH.name: KGFHeader
src/kgf/parser/parse_kgf.mbt::value:KGFH.doc: KGF header information for fast filtering.

src/kgf/parser/parse_kgf.mbt::value:pars.name: parse_kgf_header
src/kgf/parser/parse_kgf.mbt::value:pars.doc:
Fast header-only parsing for filtering specs without full parse.
 Only extracts language and sources from the header.

src/kgf/parser/parse_kgf.mbt::value:pars.name: parse_kgf
src/kgf/parser/parse_kgf.mbt::value:pars.doc:
Parses a full KGF document text into a structured spec.

 Only header, features, resolver, and ignore are parsed eagerly.
 Heavy sections (lex, grammar, attrs, semantics, preprocess) are
 stored as raw text and parsed lazily on first access.

src/kgf/parser/parse_kgf.mbt::value:pars.name: parse_features
src/kgf/parser/parse_kgf.mbt::value:pars.doc:
Parses feature metadata section into name -> values map.
 Each line is `name: value1, value2`.

src/kgf/parser/parse_kgf.mbt::value:pars.name: parse_ignore
src/kgf/parser/parse_kgf.mbt::value:pars.doc:
Parses ignore section into list of patterns.
 Each line is a pattern (like .gitignore syntax).
 Lines starting with # are comments.

src/kgf/parser/parse_kgf.mbt::value:pars.name: parse_preprocess
src/kgf/parser/parse_kgf.mbt::value:pars.doc: Parses preprocess section into list of transducer names.

src/kgf/parser/parse_classify.mbt::value.name: parse_classify
src/kgf/parser/parse_classify.mbt::value.doc:
Parses the `=== classify` section of a KGF spec.

 The classify section contains a trained classification model persisted
 by a training tool (e.g., `indexion classify train`). The weights are
 computed from a document corpus, NOT hand-authored.

 ## Syntax

 ```
 # Metadata: key = value (model parameters, corpus statistics)
 model_type = naive_bayes
 corpus_size = 150
 smoothing = laplace

 # Class definition: @class_name prior=log_probability
 # Followed by indented term=weight lines (conditional log-probabilities)
 @normative prior=-0.693
   shall=-0.105
   must=-0.223
   required=-0.511

 @meta prior=-1.204
   introduction=-0.693
   scope=-1.099
 ```

 Lines starting with `#` are comments. Blank lines are ignored.

src/kgf/parser/parse_classify.mbt::value.name: parse_class_header
src/kgf/parser/parse_classify.mbt::value.doc:
Parses a class header: `class_name prior=value`
 Returns (class_name, prior_value).

src/kgf/parser/parse_classify.mbt::value.name: find_last_eq
src/kgf/parser/parse_classify.mbt::value.doc:
Finds the last `=` in a string. Used to separate term from weight
 in case the term itself contains `=`.

src/kgf/parser/parse_classify.mbt::value.name: parse_double
src/kgf/parser/parse_classify.mbt::value.doc:
Parses a decimal number string into a Double.
 Handles integers, decimals, and negative numbers.

src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:split_li.name: split_lines
src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:split_li.doc: Splits text into lines, handling both \n and \r\n line endings.

src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:is_white.name: is_whitespace
src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:is_white.doc: Checks if a character is whitespace.

src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:is_digit.name: is_digit
src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:is_digit.doc: Checks if a character is a digit.

src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:is_ident.name: is_ident_start
src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:is_ident.doc: Checks if a character is an identifier start character.

src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:is_ident.name: is_ident_char
src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:is_ident.doc: Checks if a character is an identifier character.

src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:find_sub.name: find_substring
src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:find_sub.doc:
Finds the index of a substring in a string.
 Returns -1 if not found.

src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:find_lin.name: find_line_start_substring
src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:find_lin.doc:
Finds the index of a substring that appears at the start of a line.
 A line start is either position 0 or immediately after a newline character.
 Returns -1 if not found.

src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:find_cha.name: find_char
src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:find_cha.doc:
Finds the index of a character in a string starting from a position.
 Returns -1 if not found.

src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:starts_w.name: starts_with
src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:starts_w.doc: Checks if a string starts with a given prefix.

src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:starts_w.name: starts_with_at
src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:starts_w.doc: Checks if a string starts with a given prefix at a specific position.

src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:trim_str.name: trim_str
src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:trim_str.doc: Trims whitespace from both ends of a string.

src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:parse_li.name: parse_list
src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:parse_li.doc: Splits a comma/whitespace list into string array.

src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:substr.name: substr
src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:substr.doc: Gets a substring from start to end position.

src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:substr_f.name: substr_from
src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:substr_f.doc: Gets a substring from start to the end of the string.

src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:char_at.name: char_at
src/kgf/parser/utils.mbt::value:char_at.doc: Gets a character at a position, returning Char.

parse_on_blocks

src/kgf/parser/parse_semantics.mbt::valu.name: read_ident_at

src/kgf/parser/parse_semantics.mbt::valu.name: read_brace_block

src/kgf/parser/parse_semantics.mbt::valu.name: parse_block_stmts
src/kgf/parser/parse_semantics.mbt::valu.doc: Parses statements inside a block.

src/kgf/parser/parse_semantics.mbt::valu.name: parse_stmt
src/kgf/parser/parse_semantics.mbt::valu.doc: Parses a single statement.

src/kgf/parser/parse_semantics.mbt::valu.name: parse_edge_stmt
src/kgf/parser/parse_semantics.mbt::valu.doc: Parses: edge kind from <expr> to <expr> [attrs <expr>]

src/kgf/parser/parse_semantics.mbt::valu.name: parse_bind_stmt
src/kgf/parser/parse_semantics.mbt::valu.doc: Parses: bind ns <expr> name <expr> to <expr>

src/kgf/parser/parse_semantics.mbt::valu.name: parse_note_stmt

src/kgf/parser/parse_semantics.mbt::valu.name: find_keyword
src/kgf/parser/parse_semantics.mbt::valu.doc: Finds a keyword position, ensuring it's a separate word.

src/kgf/parser/parse_semantics.mbt::valu.name: parse_expr
src/kgf/parser/parse_semantics.mbt::valu.doc: Parses an expression.

src/kgf/parser/parse_semantics.mbt::valu.name: unescape_string_literal
is_number
src/kgf/parser/parse_semantics.mbt::valu.doc: Checks if a string represents a number.

src/kgf/parser/parse_semantics.mbt::valu.name: parse_number
src/kgf/parser/parse_semantics.mbt::valu.doc: Parses a number string to double.

src/kgf/parser/parse_semantics.mbt::valu.name: is_identifier
src/kgf/parser/parse_semantics.mbt::valu.doc: Checks if a string is a valid identifier.

src/kgf/parser/parse_semantics.mbt::valu.name: has_call_syntax

src/kgf/parser/parse_rules.mbt::value:jo.name: join_rule_buffer

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:Gramma.name: GrammarToken

Token types for the PEG expression tokenizer.

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:ParseS.name: ParseState
src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:ParseS.doc: Parser state holding tokens and current position.

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:new.name: new

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:has_mo.name: has_more

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:advanc.name: advance

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:eat_id.name: eat_ident

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:eat_lp.name: eat_lp
