src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:eat_rp.name: eat_rp
src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:eat_rp.doc: Checks if current token is RP and consumes it.

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:eat_lb.name: eat_lb
src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:eat_lb.doc: Checks if current token is LB and consumes it.

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:eat_rb.name: eat_rb
src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:eat_rb.doc: Checks if current token is RB and consumes it.

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:eat_st.name: eat_star
src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:eat_st.doc: Checks if current token is STAR and consumes it.

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:eat_pl.name: eat_plus
src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:eat_pl.doc: Checks if current token is PLUS and consumes it.

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:eat_q.name: eat_q
src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:eat_q.doc: Checks if current token is Q (?) and consumes it.

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:eat_ba.name: eat_bar
src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:eat_ba.doc: Checks if current token is BAR and consumes it.

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:eat_co.name: eat_colon
src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:eat_co.doc: Checks if current token is COLON and consumes it.

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:eat_am.name: eat_amp
src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:eat_am.doc: Checks if current token is AMP (&) and consumes it.

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:eat_ex.name: eat_excl
src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:eat_ex.doc: Checks if current token is EXCL (!) and consumes it.

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:eat_sl.name: eat_slash
src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:eat_sl.doc: Checks if current token is SLASH (/) and consumes it.

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:is_sto.name: is_stop_token
src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:is_sto.doc: Returns true if current token is a stop token (RP, RB, BAR, SLASH).

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:is_ide.name: is_ident_start
src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:is_ide.doc: Checks if a character is a valid identifier start.

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:is_ide.name: is_ident_char
src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:is_ide.doc: Checks if a character is a valid identifier continuation.

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:is_whi.name: is_whitespace
src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:is_whi.doc: Checks if a character is whitespace.

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:remove.name: remove_comment
src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:remove.doc: Removes comments from a line (everything after #).

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:prepro.name: preprocess
src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:prepro.doc: Preprocesses the input string: removes comments and empty lines.

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:tokeni.name: tokenize_expr
src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:tokeni.doc: Tokenizes a PEG expression string into grammar tokens.

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:parse_.name: parse_primary
src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:parse_.doc: Parses a primary expression (identifier, label, parenthesized group, bracketed optional, or predicate).

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:parse_.name: parse_postfix
src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:parse_.doc: Parses a postfix expression (primary with optional *, +, or ?).

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:parse_.name: parse_choice
src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:parse_.doc:
Parses a choice expression (alternatives separated by / or |).
 PEG standard uses /. The | operator is deprecated.

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:parse_.name: parse_expr
src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:parse_.doc: Parses a PEG expression string into a Node AST.

src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:parse_.name: parse_expr_with_warnings
src/kgf/peg/parse_expr.mbt::value:parse_.doc: Parses a PEG expression string into a Node AST, returning deprecation warnings.

src/kgf/peg/eval_recursive.mbt::value:sh.name: should_emit_rule_rec
src/kgf/peg/eval_recursive.mbt::value:sh.doc: Check if rule should be emitted.

src/kgf/peg/eval.mbt::value:EvalResult.name: EvalResult
src/kgf/peg/eval.mbt::value:EvalResult.doc: EvalResult represents the result of a PEG evaluation.

src/kgf/peg/eval.mbt::value:is_ok.name: is_ok
src/kgf/peg/eval.mbt::value:is_ok.doc: Returns true if the evaluation succeeded.

src/kgf/peg/eval.mbt::value:get_label.name: get_label
src/kgf/peg/eval.mbt::value:get_label.doc: Gets a label value by name.

src/kgf/peg/eval_iter.mbt::value:WorkIte.name: WorkItem
src/kgf/peg/eval_iter.mbt::value:WorkIte.doc:
Work item for the evaluation stack.
 Uses Labels for lazy label evaluation.

src/kgf/peg/eval_iter.mbt::value:should_.name: should_emit_rule_iter
src/kgf/peg/eval_iter.mbt::value:should_.doc: Check if rule should be emitted.

src/kgf/peg/eval_iter.mbt::value:record_.name: record_token_failure
src/kgf/peg/eval_iter.mbt::value:record_.doc: Records a failed token expectation for diagnostic runs.

src/kgf/peg/eval_iter.mbt::value:process.name: process_predicate_cont
src/kgf/peg/eval_iter.mbt::value:process.doc:
Process And/Not-predicate continuation (unified).
 When negate is false, behaves as And-predicate; when true, as Not-predicate.

src/kgf/peg/peg.mbt::value:PEG.name: PEG
src/kgf/peg/peg.mbt::value:PEG.doc: PEG represents a compiled PEG grammar with parsed rule ASTs.

src/kgf/peg/peg.mbt::value:is_token.name: is_token
src/kgf/peg/peg.mbt::value:is_token.doc: Checks if a name is a token.

src/kgf/peg/peg.mbt::value:get_rule.name: get_rule
src/kgf/peg/peg.mbt::value:get_rule.doc: Gets the AST node for a specific rule.

src/kgf/peg/peg.mbt::value:get_token_id.name: get_token_id
src/kgf/peg/peg.mbt::value:get_token_id.doc: Gets the token ID for a token name.

src/kgf/peg/peg.mbt::value:get_fast_rule.name: get_fast_rule_body_id
src/kgf/peg/peg.mbt::value:get_fast_rule.doc: Gets the body node id for a rule (without FRuleRef wrapper).

src/kgf/peg/peg.mbt::value:get_rule_attr.name: get_rule_attrs
src/kgf/peg/peg.mbt::value:get_rule_attr.doc: Gets the attribute actions for a specific rule.

src/kgf/peg/peg.mbt::value:compile_node.name: compile_node
src/kgf/peg/peg.mbt::value:compile_node.doc: Compiles an AST node into ID-based node table for faster evaluation.

src/kgf/peg/eval_common.mbt::value:Label.name: Labels
src/kgf/peg/eval_common.mbt::value:Label.doc:
Lazy labels: defer Map creation until actually needed.
 TokenRef stores token index - only materialize when labels are accessed.

src/kgf/peg/eval_common.mbt::value:MemoS.name: MemoState
src/kgf/peg/eval_common.mbt::value:MemoS.doc:
Memo entry states for memoization.
 Uses Labels for efficient storage.

src/kgf/peg/eval_common.mbt::value:FastE.name: FastEvalResult
src/kgf/peg/eval_common.mbt::value:FastE.doc: FastEvalResult holds evaluation result with lazy labels.

src/kgf/peg/eval_common.mbt::value:full_.name: full_labels
src/kgf/peg/eval_common.mbt::value:full_.doc: Creates full labels from a Map.

src/kgf/peg/eval_common.mbt::value:clone.name: clone_labels
src/kgf/peg/eval_common.mbt::value:clone.doc: Clones labels (cheap for NoLabels and TokRef).

src/kgf/peg/eval_common.mbt::value:compu.name: compute_token_names
src/kgf/peg/eval_common.mbt::value:compu.doc: Pre-computes token names by token ID for diagnostics.

src/kgf/peg/eval_common.mbt::value:creat.name: create_memo
src/kgf/peg/eval_common.mbt::value:creat.doc: Creates memoization array for PEG evaluation.

src/kgf/peg/diagnostics.mbt::value:Parse.name: ParseDiagnostic
src/kgf/peg/diagnostics.mbt::value:Parse.doc: ParseDiagnostic describes where PEG parsing stopped and what was expected.

src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:Node.name: Node
src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:Node.doc:
PEG AST Node types representing the parsed grammar expression.
 Conforms to Bryan Ford's PEG specification (POPL 2004).

src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:CompiledNode.name: CompiledNode
src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:CompiledNode.doc:
CompiledNode is an ID-based representation for evaluation hot paths.
 Note: This is legacy code superseded by FastCompiledNode but kept for API compatibility.

src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:FastCompiled.name: FastCompiledNode
src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:FastCompiled.doc:
FastCompiledNode separates tokens and rules for O(1) matching.
 Token IDs and rule node IDs are pre-computed at compile time.

src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:is_empty.name: is_empty
src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:is_empty.doc: Returns true if this node is Empty.

src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:is_sym.name: is_sym
src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:is_sym.doc: Returns true if this node is a Symbol.

src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:get_sym.name: get_sym
src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:get_sym.doc: Gets the symbol name if this is a Sym node.

src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:is_seq.name: is_seq
src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:is_seq.doc: Returns true if this node is a Sequence.

src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:get_seq.name: get_seq
src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:get_seq.doc: Gets the sequence elements if this is a Seq node.

src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:is_choice.name: is_choice
src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:is_choice.doc: Returns true if this node is a Choice.

src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:get_choice.name: get_choice
src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:get_choice.doc: Gets the choice alternatives if this is a Choice node.

src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:is_star.name: is_star
src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:is_star.doc: Returns true if this node is a Star (zero or more).

src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:get_star.name: get_star
src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:get_star.doc: Gets the inner node if this is a Star node.

src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:is_plus.name: is_plus
src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:is_plus.doc: Returns true if this node is a Plus (one or more).

src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:get_plus.name: get_plus
src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:get_plus.doc: Gets the inner node if this is a Plus node.

src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:is_optional.name: is_optional
src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:is_optional.doc: Returns true if this node is Optional (zero or one).

src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:get_optional.name: get_optional
src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:get_optional.doc: Gets the inner node if this is an Optional node.

src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:is_label.name: is_label
src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:is_label.doc: Returns true if this node is a Label.

src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:get_label.name: get_label
src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:get_label.doc: Gets the label name and inner node if this is a Label node.

src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:is_and.name: is_and
src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:is_and.doc: Returns true if this node is an And predicate (positive lookahead).

src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:get_and.name: get_and
src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:get_and.doc: Gets the inner node if this is an And node.

src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:is_not.name: is_not
src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:is_not.doc: Returns true if this node is a Not predicate (negative lookahead).

src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:get_not.name: get_not
src/kgf/peg/node.mbt::value:get_not.doc: Gets the inner node if this is a Not node.

src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:KGF.name: KGFRegistry
src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:KGF.doc: KGFRegistry holds loaded specs and provides lookup functions.

src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:loa.name: load_from_dir
src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:loa.doc: Load all KGF specs from a directory (recursively including subdirectories).

src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:loa.name: load_specs_recursive
src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:loa.doc: Recursively load KGF specs from a directory.

src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:get.name: get_lang_from_ext
src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:get.doc: Get language name from file extension (with or without dot).

src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:get.name: get_doc_spec_name
src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:get.doc: Get the doc spec name for a language (returns language-doc if exists, otherwise the language itself).

src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:sup.name: supports_ext
src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:sup.doc: Check if an extension is supported.

src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:is_.name: is_file_supported
src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:is_.doc:
Check if a file path is supported by any registered spec.
 Handles both extension-based matching (e.g., ".ts") and filename-based matching (e.g., "moon.pkg").

src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:det.name: detect_from_path
src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:det.doc:
Detect language from file path.
 Handles both extension-based patterns (e.g., ".ts") and filename-based patterns (e.g., "moon.pkg").

src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:spe.name: spec_count
src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:spe.doc: Get total number of registered specs.

src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:to_.name: to_specs_map
src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:to_.doc: Get a copy of all loaded specs as language -> spec map.

src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:get.name: get_external_prefixes
src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:get.doc:
Get all external prefixes (bare_prefix values) from registered specs.
 These prefixes indicate external dependencies (e.g., "pkg:", "npm:", "pip:").

src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:get.name: get_project_markers
src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:get.doc:
Get project/root marker patterns derived from loaded KGF specs.
 This collects filename-based `sources`, resolver `project_config`, and
 `manifest` / `marker` resolve steps so callers do not hardcode ecosystem
 manifests.

src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:col.name: collect_ignore_patterns
src/kgf/registry/registry.mbt::value:col.doc:
Collect all ignore patterns from loaded KGF specs.
 Returns (pattern, language) pairs.

src/kgf/manage/list.mbt::value:get_insta.name: get_installed_kgfs_dir
src/kgf/manage/list.mbt::value:get_insta.doc: Resolve the installed kgfs directory using the config SoT.

src/kgf/manage/list.mbt::value:list_inst.name: list_installed_specs
src/kgf/manage/list.mbt::value:list_inst.doc:
List all installed specs with metadata.
 Category is derived from the subdirectory name (not hardcoded).

src/kgf/manage/list.mbt::value:collect_s.name: collect_specs_recursive
src/kgf/manage/list.mbt::value:collect_s.doc: Recursively scan directory for .kgf files and extract metadata.

src/kgf/manage/list.mbt::value:extract_s.name: extract_spec_info
src/kgf/manage/list.mbt::value:extract_s.doc: Extract spec metadata from a .kgf file using header-only parse.

src/kgf/manage/list.mbt::value:derive_ca.name: derive_category
src/kgf/manage/list.mbt::value:derive_ca.doc:
Derive category from relative path between file and kgfs root.
 e.g., root="/data/kgfs", path="/data/kgfs/programming/go.kgf" → "programming"

src/kgf/manage/list.mbt::value:sort_spec.name: sort_specs
src/kgf/manage/list.mbt::value:sort_spec.doc: Sort specs by category then name.

src/kgf/manage/download.mbt::value:githu.name: github_headers
src/kgf/manage/download.mbt::value:githu.doc: Common HTTP headers for GitHub API requests.

src/kgf/manage/download.mbt::value:find_.name: find_extracted_dir
src/kgf/manage/download.mbt::value:find_.doc: Find the extracted directory inside the temp dir (GitHub tarball prefix).

src/kgf/manage/download.mbt::value:find_.name: find_spec_in_tree
src/kgf/manage/download.mbt::value:find_.doc: Search the GitHub tree API response for a file matching the target filename.

src/kgf/manage/types.mbt::value:KgfSpecI.name: KgfSpecInfo
src/kgf/manage/types.mbt::value:KgfSpecI.doc: Metadata about an installed KGF spec.

src/kgf/check/source_map.mbt::value:Sour.name: SourceMap
src/kgf/check/source_map.mbt::value:Sour.doc:
SourceMap maps byte offsets in a source string to 1-based line and column
 coordinates.

src/kgf/check/source_map.mbt::value:new.name: new
src/kgf/check/source_map.mbt::value:new.doc:
Builds a source map by recording the byte offset where each line begins.
 Both LF and CRLF line endings are treated as one line break.

src/kgf/check/source_map.mbt::value:reso.name: resolve
src/kgf/check/source_map.mbt::value:reso.doc: Resolves a byte offset to a 1-based `(line, column)` pair.

src/kgf/check/check.mbt::value:Diagnosti.name: Diagnostic
src/kgf/check/check.mbt::value:Diagnosti.doc: A single diagnostic produced by validation.

src/kgf/check/check.mbt::value:extract_s.name: extract_symbols_from_expr
src/kgf/check/check.mbt::value:extract_s.doc:
Extract symbol names referenced in a PEG expression string.
 Symbols are uppercase-starting identifiers (tokens) or any identifier
 that isn't a PEG operator.

src/kgf/check/check.mbt::value:is_symbol.name: is_symbol_candidate
src/kgf/check/check.mbt::value:is_symbol.doc:
Returns true if a word looks like a grammar symbol reference
 (not a PEG operator keyword or label).

src/kgf/check/check.mbt::value:is_known_.name: is_known_builtin
src/kgf/check/check.mbt::value:is_known_.doc:
Returns true if `name` is a known function in the KGF semantic DSL.
 Delegates to the SoT in the semantics package (eval_expr.mbt).

src/kgf/check/check_diagnostic.mbt::valu.name: CheckDiagnostic
src/kgf/check/check_diagnostic.mbt::valu.doc: CheckDiagnostic is the unified diagnostic emitted by `indexion check`.

src/kgf/check/check_diagnostic.mbt::valu.name: new
src/kgf/check/check_diagnostic.mbt::valu.doc: Creates a check diagnostic with file and resolved source position.

src/kgf/check/check_diagnostic.mbt::valu.name: compare
src/kgf/check/check_diagnostic.mbt::valu.doc: Compares diagnostics by file, line, then column for stable output ordering.

src/kgf/check/check_diagnostic.mbt::valu.name: format_human
src/kgf/check/check_diagnostic.mbt::valu.doc: Formats a diagnostic in standard compiler style.

src/kgf/check/check_diagnostic.mbt::valu.name: diagnostics_to_json
src/kgf/check/check_diagnostic.mbt::valu.doc: Converts an array of check diagnostics to a JSON string.

src/kgf/tokenize/tokenize.mbt::value:fea.name: feature_has_value
src/kgf/tokenize/tokenize.mbt::value:fea.doc: Check whether a feature string carries a value (contains `:` separator).

src/kgf/tokenize/tokenize.mbt::value:fea.name: feature_value
src/kgf/tokenize/tokenize.mbt::value:fea.doc:
Extract the value part from a feature string.
 "Ident:config" → "config"; "KW_fn" → "KW_fn"

src/kgf/tokenize/tokenize.mbt::value:fea.name: feature_kind
src/kgf/tokenize/tokenize.mbt::value:fea.doc:
Extract the kind part from a feature string.
 "Ident:config" → "Ident"; "KW_fn" → "KW_fn"

src/kgf/tokenize/tokenize.mbt::value:sub.name: sub_tokenize_vocab
src/kgf/tokenize/tokenize.mbt::value:sub.doc:
Split text into lowercase words and emit "vocab:" prefixed feature strings.
 Words shorter than 3 characters are discarded.

 Used for vocabulary_token_kinds content (doc comments, string literals,
 natural language text) to enable vocabulary-level comparison.

src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit.mbt::value:Langu.name: LanguageToolkit
src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit.mbt::value:Langu.doc:
LanguageToolkit caches all runtime artifacts derived from a KGFSpec.

 Creating a Lexer, building feature sets (identifier kinds, vocabulary kinds,
 qualifier separators, etc.) from spec features — these are pure derivations
 that never change for a given spec. Rather than letting each consumer rebuild
 them on every file, LanguageToolkit constructs them once and exposes the
 cached results.

 Obtain a toolkit via `ToolkitRegistry::get(lang)` or the convenience
 `ToolkitRegistry::for_path(path)`.

src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit.mbt::value:get_l.name: get_lexer
src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit.mbt::value:get_l.doc: The compiled lexer for this language (built once from spec tokens).

src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit.mbt::value:get_i.name: get_identifier_set
src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit.mbt::value:get_i.doc: Token kinds that represent identifiers (e.g. Ident, TypeIdent).

src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit.mbt::value:get_v.name: get_vocabulary_set
src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit.mbt::value:get_v.doc:
Token kinds whose text content should be sub-tokenized for vocabulary
 comparison (e.g. doc comments, string literals).

src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit.mbt::value:get_a.name: get_allowed_keywords
src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit.mbt::value:get_a.doc: Keywords allowed in proxy function bodies (function_keyword + self_keyword).

src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit.mbt::value:get_q.name: get_qualifier_separators
src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit.mbt::value:get_q.doc: Qualifier separator token kinds (DOT, DCOLON, SCOPE, etc.).

src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit.mbt::value:token.name: tokenize
src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit.mbt::value:token.doc: Tokenize source content using the cached lexer.

src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit.mbt::value:prepr.name: preprocess_and_tokenize
src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit.mbt::value:prepr.doc: Apply preprocessing steps from the spec to content, then tokenize.

src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit.mbt::value:prepr.name: preprocess
src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit.mbt::value:prepr.doc:
Apply preprocessing steps from the spec to content.
 Returns content unchanged if no preprocess section exists.

src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit.mbt::value:is_id.name: is_identifier
src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit.mbt::value:is_id.doc: Check if a token kind is an identifier.

src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit.mbt::value:is_ke.name: is_keyword
src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit.mbt::value:is_ke.doc: Check if a token kind is a keyword.

src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit_registry.mbt::va.name: ToolkitRegistry
src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit_registry.mbt::va.doc:
ToolkitRegistry wraps a KGFRegistry and caches LanguageToolkit instances
 per language. This ensures that derived artifacts (Lexer, identifier sets,
 etc.) are built exactly once per language, regardless of how many files
 of that language are processed.

src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit_registry.mbt::va.name: get
src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit_registry.mbt::va.doc:
Get the LanguageToolkit for a language name.
 Builds and caches on first access.

src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit_registry.mbt::va.name: for_path
src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit_registry.mbt::va.doc:
Detect language from file path and return the corresponding toolkit.
 Convenience method combining detect_from_path + get.

src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit_registry.mbt::va.name: tokenize
src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit_registry.mbt::va.doc:
Tokenize source content for a file path.
 Returns None if no spec matches the file.

src/kgf/resolver/module_path.mbt::value:.name: replace_all
src/kgf/resolver/module_path.mbt::value:.doc: Replaces all occurrences of a substring with another substring.

src/kgf/resolver/module_path.mbt::value:.name: starts_with_at_arr
src/kgf/resolver/module_path.mbt::value:.doc: Checks if a char array starts with a pattern at a given position.

src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:Fil.name: FileResolver
src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:Fil.doc:
FileResolver trait for checking file existence and reading files.
 Implementations should provide the actual file system access.

src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:str.name: strip_relative_prefix
src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:str.doc: Strips relative prefixes (./, ../) from module string.

src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:que.name: query_manifest
src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:que.doc:
Query a manifest file (package.json, Cargo.toml) for an entry point.
 Supports simple JSON path queries like "main", "exports['.'].import".

src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:que.name: query_json_simple
src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:que.doc:
Simple JSON query for common manifest fields.
 Supports: "main", "module", "exports['.'].import", etc.

src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:res.name: resolve_json_path
src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:res.doc:
Resolve a dot/bracket path against a parsed JSON value.
 Supports: "main", "exports[\".\"].import", "exports[\".\"]"

src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:spl.name: split_json_path
src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:spl.doc: Split a JSON path like `exports["."].import` into segments ["exports", ".", "import"].

src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:fin.name: find_json_string_value
src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:fin.doc: Find a simple string value in JSON content.

src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:spl.name: split_by_char
src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:spl.doc: Split a string by a character.

src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:nor.name: normalize_relative_result
src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:nor.doc: Normalizes a result path to be relative to project root.

src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:has.name: has_relative_prefix
src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:has.doc: Checks if a module path has any of the relative prefixes.

src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:get.name: get_module_head
src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:get.doc: Gets the head part of a module (before first . or :: or /).

src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:con.name: contains_char
src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:con.doc: Checks if a string contains a specific character.

src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:joi.name: join_with_backslash
src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:joi.doc: Joins array elements with backslash up to a count.

src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:fin.name: find_char_pos
src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:fin.doc: Finds the position of a character in a string.

src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:min.name: min_int
src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:min.doc: Returns the minimum of two integers.

src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:FnF.name: FnFileResolver
src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:FnF.doc: FnFileResolver wraps functions as a FileResolver.

src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:Res.name: ResolutionResult
src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:Res.doc: Resolution result indicating success or circular dependency.

src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:to_.name: to_string
src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:to_.doc:
Converts a ResolutionResult to a string for JSON serialization.
 Returns the resolved path, or a sentinel value for circular dependencies.

src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:is_.name: is_circular_sentinel
src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:is_.doc: Checks if a resolved path is a circular dependency sentinel.

src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:par.name: parse_circular_sentinel
src/kgf/resolver/resolver.mbt::value:par.doc:
Parses a circular sentinel to extract (from_file, module_path).
 Returns None if not a valid sentinel.

src/kgf/resolver/alias.mbt::value:apply_.name: apply_single_alias
src/kgf/resolver/alias.mbt::value:apply_.doc:
Applies a single alias pattern to a module path.
 Supports simple prefix patterns and string replacement.

src/kgf/resolver/alias.mbt::value:replac.name: replace_first
src/kgf/resolver/alias.mbt::value:replac.doc: Replaces the first occurrence of a pattern with a replacement.

src/kgf/resolver/alias.mbt::value:find_s.name: find_substring
src/kgf/resolver/alias.mbt::value:find_s.doc:
Finds the position of a substring in a string.
 Returns -1 if not found.

src/kgf/resolver/path_utils.mbt::value:j.name: join_path
src/kgf/resolver/path_utils.mbt::value:j.doc:
Joins path parts into a single path string.
 Parts are joined with "/" separator. Empty parts are filtered out.

src/kgf/resolver/path_utils.mbt::value:d.name: dirname
src/kgf/resolver/path_utils.mbt::value:d.doc:
Returns the directory name of a path.
 For example, dirname("/a/b/c.txt") returns "/a/b"

src/kgf/resolver/path_utils.mbt::value:b.name: basename
src/kgf/resolver/path_utils.mbt::value:b.doc:
Returns the base name of a path.
 For example, basename("/a/b/c.txt") returns "c.txt"

src/kgf/resolver/path_utils.mbt::value:n.name: normalize_path
src/kgf/resolver/path_utils.mbt::value:n.doc: Normalizes a path by removing redundant slashes and resolving . and ..

src/kgf/resolver/path_utils.mbt::value:r.name: relative_path
src/kgf/resolver/path_utils.mbt::value:r.doc:
Computes the relative path from one path to another.
 Both paths should be absolute or both relative.

src/kgf/resolver/path_utils.mbt::value:i.name: is_absolute
src/kgf/resolver/path_utils.mbt::value:i.doc: Checks if a path is absolute (starts with /).

src/kgf/resolver/path_utils.mbt::value:f.name: find_last_char
src/kgf/resolver/path_utils.mbt::value:f.doc:
Finds the last occurrence of a character in a string.
 Returns -1 if not found.

src/kgf/resolver/path_utils.mbt::value:s.name: split_string
src/kgf/resolver/path_utils.mbt::value:s.doc: Splits a string by a delimiter character.

src/kgf/resolver/path_utils.mbt::value:j.name: join_array_strings
src/kgf/resolver/path_utils.mbt::value:j.doc: Joins an array of strings with a separator.

src/vfs/vfs.mbt::value:VFSEntry.name: VFSEntry
src/vfs/vfs.mbt::value:VFSEntry.doc: A virtual file entry — either a real file or an entry inside an archive.

src/vfs/vfs.mbt::value:ArchiveSpec.name: ArchiveSpec
src/vfs/vfs.mbt::value:ArchiveSpec.doc:
Configuration for archive expansion.
 Derived from KGF spec features (content_type: archive).

src/vfs/vfs.mbt::value:expand_archive.name: expand_archive
src/vfs/vfs.mbt::value:expand_archive.doc:
Expand a single archive file into VFSEntry items.
 Text entries (UTF-8 decodable) get their content as-is.
 Binary entries get base64-encoded content.

src/vfs/vfs.mbt::value:extract_text_cont.name: extract_text_content
src/vfs/vfs.mbt::value:extract_text_cont.doc:
Extract concatenated text content from an archive file.
 Returns all non-binary inner entries joined by newlines.
 This is the SoT for "read an archive as a single document".

src/vfs/vfs.mbt::value:extract_text_entr.name: extract_text_entries
src/vfs/vfs.mbt::value:extract_text_entr.doc:
Extract individual text entries from an archive as (virtual_path, content) pairs.
 This is the SoT for "enumerate an archive's text files for per-file comparison".

src/vfs/vfs.mbt::value:is_virtual_path.name: is_virtual_path
src/vfs/vfs.mbt::value:is_virtual_path.doc:
Check if a path is a virtual (archive-internal) path.
 Virtual paths contain the "!/" separator (e.g. "report.pptx!/ppt/slides/slide1.xml").

src/vfs/vfs.mbt::value:parse_virtual_pat.name: parse_virtual_path
src/vfs/vfs.mbt::value:parse_virtual_pat.doc:
Parse a virtual path into (archive_path, inner_path) if it contains the archive separator.
 Returns None for regular file paths.

src/vfs/vfs.mbt::value:inner_path.name: inner_path
src/vfs/vfs.mbt::value:inner_path.doc:
Extract just the inner path portion from a virtual path.
 For regular paths, returns the path itself.

src/parallel/parallel.mbt::value:availab.name: available
src/parallel/parallel.mbt::value:availab.doc: Whether fork-based parallelism is available on this platform.

src/parallel/parallel.mbt::value:cpu_cou.name: cpu_count
src/parallel/parallel.mbt::value:cpu_cou.doc: Return the number of available CPU cores.

src/parallel/parallel.mbt::value:exit.name: exit
src/parallel/parallel.mbt::value:exit.doc: Exit the current process immediately with the given status code.

src/reconcile/plan/index_db.mbt::value:a.name: allocate_vector_id
src/reconcile/plan/index_db.mbt::value:a.doc: Allocate the next vector ID from the manifest counter.

src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Reco.name: ReconcileConfig
src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Reco.doc: CLI and file-backed configuration for the reconcile command.

src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:defa.name: default
src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:defa.doc: Create a default reconcile configuration with sensible defaults.

src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Code.name: CodeUnit
src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Code.doc: A code symbol extracted from the dependency graph for reconciliation.

src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:DocF.name: DocFragment
src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:DocF.doc: A fragment of documentation extracted from a document file.

src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Code.name: CodeAnchor
src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Code.doc: Anchor referencing a code symbol within a reconcile candidate.

src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:DocA.name: DocAnchor
src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:DocA.doc: Anchor referencing a documentation fragment within a reconcile candidate.

src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Mapp.name: MappingEvidence
src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Mapp.doc: Evidence supporting the code-to-doc mapping for a candidate.

src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Reco.name: ReconcileCandidate
src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Reco.doc: A candidate representing a code symbol that may need documentation updates.

src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Logi.name: LogicalReviewTask
src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Logi.doc: A queued task for human logical review of a reconcile candidate.

src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Reco.name: ReconcileSummary
src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Reco.doc: Aggregate statistics summarizing the reconcile report.

src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Cach.name: CacheState
src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Cach.doc: Cache hit/miss state and fingerprint info for incremental runs.

src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Reco.name: ReconcileReportConfig
src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Reco.doc: Snapshot of configuration settings stored within the reconcile report.

src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Reco.name: ReconcileAggregateSuggestion
src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Reco.doc: Grouped suggestion aggregating multiple candidates at module or project scope.

src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Voca.name: VocabularyDivergence
src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Voca.doc:
Vocabulary-level divergence between a source module and its documentation.
 Complements symbol-level matching by comparing TF-IDF vocabulary distributions
 to detect terms present in source code but absent from documentation.

src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Reco.name: ReconcileReport
src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Reco.doc: Full reconcile report output including candidates, reviews, and summary.

src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Inde.name: IndexedRecordEntry
src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Inde.doc: An entry in the index database tracking a record's digest and vector ID.

src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Logi.name: LogicalReviewEntry
src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Logi.doc: Persisted state of a logical review decision in the manifest.

src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Revi.name: ReviewDecisionInput
src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Revi.doc: User-supplied review decision for a logical review task.

src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Reco.name: ReconcileManifest
src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:Reco.doc: Persistent manifest tracking indexed records, reviews, and fingerprints.

src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:empt.name: empty
src/reconcile/plan/types.mbt::value:empt.doc: Create an empty manifest with default version and zero counters.

src/reconcile/plan/analysis.mbt::value:A.name: AnalysisResult
src/reconcile/plan/analysis.mbt::value:A.doc: Core analysis result — discriminated by whether cache was hit.

src/reconcile/plan/analysis.mbt::value:F.name: FreshAnalysisState
src/reconcile/plan/analysis.mbt::value:F.doc: State from a full (non-cached) analysis, needed for CLI cache persistence.

src/reconcile/plan/mapping.mbt::value:Un.name: UnitMatchTerms
src/reconcile/plan/mapping.mbt::value:Un.doc: Pre-computed lowered search terms for a code unit.

src/reconcile/plan/mapping.mbt::value:ma.name: match_fragment_with_terms
src/reconcile/plan/mapping.mbt::value:ma.doc: Optimized match using pre-computed lowered text and unit terms.

src/reconcile/plan/mapping.mbt::value:un.name: unit_match_terms
src/reconcile/plan/mapping.mbt::value:un.doc: Build pre-computed match terms for a code unit.

src/reconcile/plan/mapping.mbt::value:bu.name: build_fragment_inverted_index
src/reconcile/plan/mapping.mbt::value:bu.doc:
Build inverted index: token → [fragment array index].
 Used to narrow candidate fragments for each unit.

src/reconcile/plan/vocabulary.mbt::value.name: TokenizedModuleGroup
src/reconcile/plan/vocabulary.mbt::value.doc: Tokenized module group for vocabulary comparison.

src/reconcile/plan/vocabulary.mbt::value.name: ModuleFileGroup
src/reconcile/plan/vocabulary.mbt::value.doc: A group of source files and documentation files within a directory.

src/reconcile/store/io.mbt::value:ensure.name: ensure_cache_dir
src/reconcile/store/io.mbt::value:ensure.doc:
Ensures that `cache_dir` exists and is a directory.
 Returns `Ok(())` on success, or `Err(message)` describing the failure.

src/reconcile/store/io.mbt::value:write_.name: write_json_file
src/reconcile/store/io.mbt::value:write_.doc:
Writes JSON to `path`. Returns `Ok(())` on success,
 or `Err(message)` describing the failure.

src/reconcile/store/io.mbt::value:read_j.name: read_json_file
src/reconcile/store/io.mbt::value:read_j.doc:
Reads and parses a JSON file at `path`. Returns `Ok(json)` on success,
 or `Err(message)` describing the failure.

src/regexp/parser.mbt::value:parse_unico.name: parse_unicode_escape
src/regexp/parser.mbt::value:parse_unico.doc: Parses a Unicode escape \uXXXX from the pattern. Returns (char, new_pos).

src/regexp/parser.mbt::value:parse_patte.name: parse_pattern_ctx
src/regexp/parser.mbt::value:parse_patte.doc: Parses the pattern string into a list of quantified elements.

src/regexp/parser.mbt::value:split_by_al.name: split_by_alternation
src/regexp/parser.mbt::value:split_by_al.doc: Splits a pattern by top-level alternation.

src/regexp/parser.mbt::value:find_first_.name: find_first_consuming_element
src/regexp/parser.mbt::value:find_first_.doc:
Finds the first consuming element in an element sequence,
 skipping zero-width assertions (anchors, boundaries, lookaheads).

src/regexp/parser.mbt::value:extract_fir.name: extract_first_char_ranges
src/regexp/parser.mbt::value:extract_fir.doc:
Extracts first char ranges from a single alternative's first consuming element.
 Returns None if the element can match any character (not filterable).

src/regexp/parser.mbt::value:extract_fir.name: extract_first_char_filter
src/regexp/parser.mbt::value:extract_fir.doc: Extracts a first char filter from compiled alternatives.

src/regexp/types.mbt::value:MatchResult.name: MatchResult
src/regexp/types.mbt::value:MatchResult.doc:
Result of a pattern match attempt.
 Contains whether the match succeeded, the end position, and the matched text.

src/regexp/types.mbt::value:CaptureState.name: CaptureState
src/regexp/types.mbt::value:CaptureState.doc: Mutable capture group state for tracking during matching.

src/regexp/types.mbt::value:get_group_te.name: get_group_text
src/regexp/types.mbt::value:get_group_te.doc: Get captured text for group N from input chars.

src/regexp/types.mbt::value:PatternEleme.name: PatternElement
src/regexp/types.mbt::value:PatternEleme.doc: Parsed pattern element for internal use.

src/regexp/types.mbt::value:CompiledPatt.name: CompiledPattern
src/regexp/types.mbt::value:CompiledPatt.doc: CompiledPattern stores parsed regex elements for repeated matching.

src/regexp/types.mbt::value:get_capture_.name: get_capture_count
src/regexp/types.mbt::value:get_capture_.doc: Returns the number of capture groups in the pattern.

src/regexp/types.mbt::value:FirstCharFil.name: FirstCharFilter
src/regexp/types.mbt::value:FirstCharFil.doc: First character filter for quick pattern rejection.

src/regexp/types.mbt::value:ParseContext.name: ParseContext
src/regexp/types.mbt::value:ParseContext.doc: Mutable state for pattern parsing -- tracks capture group numbering.

src/regexp/char_utils.mbt::value:is_whit.name: is_whitespace
src/regexp/char_utils.mbt::value:is_whit.doc: Checks if a character is a whitespace character.

src/regexp/char_utils.mbt::value:is_digi.name: is_digit
src/regexp/char_utils.mbt::value:is_digi.doc: Checks if a character is a digit.

src/regexp/char_utils.mbt::value:is_word.name: is_word_char
src/regexp/char_utils.mbt::value:is_word.doc: Checks if a character is a word character (alphanumeric or underscore).

src/regexp/char_utils.mbt::value:get_cha.name: get_char_at
src/regexp/char_utils.mbt::value:get_cha.doc: Gets a character from a string at the given index safely.

src/regexp/char_utils.mbt::value:build_i.name: build_input_chars
src/regexp/char_utils.mbt::value:build_i.doc: Builds a char array once for repeated indexed access during matching.

src/regexp/char_utils.mbt::value:get_inp.name: get_input_char
src/regexp/char_utils.mbt::value:get_inp.doc: Gets a character from prebuilt input chars.

src/regexp/api.mbt::value:compile_patter.name: compile_pattern
src/regexp/api.mbt::value:compile_patter.doc: Compiles a pattern once for repeated matching.

src/regexp/api.mbt::value:match_compiled.name: match_compiled_with_chars
src/regexp/api.mbt::value:match_compiled.doc: Matches a compiled pattern against the input at the given position.

src/regexp/api.mbt::value:match_pattern.name: match_pattern
src/regexp/api.mbt::value:match_pattern.doc:
Matches a pattern against the input starting at the given position.
 Returns the match result with the end position and matched text.

src/regexp/api.mbt::value:matches_at.name: matches_at
src/regexp/api.mbt::value:matches_at.doc:
Checks if a pattern matches at the start of the input at the given position.
 This is a convenience wrapper around match_pattern.

src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:match_char.name: match_char
src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:match_char.doc: Matches a single character against a pattern element.

src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:is_at_word.name: is_at_word_boundary_with_chars
src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:is_at_word.doc: Checks if position is at a word boundary using prebuilt input chars.

src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:match_quan.name: match_quantified
src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:match_quan.doc:
Matches a quantified element against the input starting at pos.
 Returns the end position if matched, or None if no match.

src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:match_simp.name: match_simple
src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:match_simp.doc: Matches a simple element (non-group) with quantifier.

src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:is_simple_.name: is_simple_element
src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:is_simple_.doc: Checks if a PatternElement can be matched with match_char (single char, no recursion).

src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:sequence_f.name: sequence_fixed_width
src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:sequence_f.doc:
Returns the fixed character width of a simple-element sequence,
 or None if any element is non-simple or has a non-One quantifier.

src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:try_match_.name: try_match_simple_sequence
src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:try_match_.doc: Matches a fixed-width sequence of simple elements without recursion.

src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:try_collec.name: try_collect_repeat_positions_fast
src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:try_collec.doc:
Fast path for repeated alternation: (A|B|C)* or (A|B|C)+
 where each alternative is a fixed-width sequence of simple elements.

 Instead of calling match_elements_backtrack recursively for each
 repetition step, this matches directly in a flat loop.

 Returns None if any alternative is not amenable (caller falls
 through to the generic backtracking path).

src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:match_grou.name: match_group_positions
src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:match_grou.doc: Returns all possible end positions for a group with a quantifier.

src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:match_grou.name: match_group
src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:match_grou.doc: Matches a group of elements (legacy -- returns first greedy match).

src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:enumerate_.name: enumerate_group_end_positions
src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:enumerate_.doc: Enumerates all possible end positions for a single group match.

src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:match_alte.name: match_alternation_positions
src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:match_alte.doc: Returns all possible end positions for an alternation with a quantifier.

src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:match_alte.name: match_alternation
src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:match_alte.doc: Matches alternation (a|b|c pattern) -- legacy, returns first greedy match.

src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:count_simp.name: count_simple_matches
src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:count_simp.doc: Counts how many consecutive characters match a simple element from pos.

src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:quantifier.name: quantifier_bounds
src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:quantifier.doc:
Returns quantifier min/max bounds. max_possible is the number of chars that
 actually match the element starting from the current position.

src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:match_elem.name: match_elements_backtrack
src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:match_elem.doc:
Matches a sequence of elements with backtracking support.
 For simple elements with variable-length quantifiers, tries different
 match lengths (greedy: max->min, lazy: min->max) and recurses on the rest.

src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:check_firs.name: check_first_char_filter
src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:check_firs.doc: Checks if a character passes the first char filter.

src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:match_comp.name: match_compiled_end_pos_with_chars
src/regexp/matcher.mbt::value:match_comp.doc: Matches a compiled pattern against the input at the given position.

src/core/graph/types.mbt::value:ModuleNo.name: ModuleNode
src/core/graph/types.mbt::value:ModuleNo.doc: Module node representing a source file or module in the codebase.

src/core/graph/types.mbt::value:SymbolNo.name: SymbolNode
src/core/graph/types.mbt::value:SymbolNo.doc: Symbol node representing a named entity (function, type, variable, etc.).

src/core/graph/types.mbt::value:EdgeKind.name: EdgeKind
src/core/graph/types.mbt::value:EdgeKind.doc: Edge kinds representing relationships between nodes.

src/core/graph/types.mbt::value:Edge.name: Edge
src/core/graph/types.mbt::value:Edge.doc: Edge representing a directed relationship between two nodes.

src/core/graph/types.mbt::value:ModuleNo.name: ModuleNote
src/core/graph/types.mbt::value:ModuleNo.doc: Note attached to a module (e.g., module_doc).

src/core/graph/types.mbt::value:CodeGrap.name: CodeGraph
src/core/graph/types.mbt::value:CodeGrap.doc: The main code graph structure containing modules, symbols, and edges.

src/core/graph/graph.mbt::value:rewrite_.name: rewrite_edge_targets
src/core/graph/graph.mbt::value:rewrite_.doc:
Rewrite edge endpoints in place according to the provided map. Used by
 post-merge enrichment to collapse external references (e.g. `pkg:<proj>/foo`)
 back to their canonical internal module ids once the project layout has
 been fully observed. The rewrite is applied to both `from` and `to` so that
 a canonicalized node participates as both source and target consistently.

src/core/graph/graph.mbt::value:get_symb.name: get_symbol

src/core/graph/graph.mbt::value:get_call.name: get_callers

src/core/graph/graph.mbt::value:to_strin.name: to_string
src/core/graph/graph.mbt::value:to_strin.doc: Convert EdgeKind to its string representation.

src/core/graph/graph.mbt::value:from_str.name: from_string
src/core/graph/graph.mbt::value:from_str.doc:

src/core/graph/graph.mbt::value:rewrite_.name: rewrite_by_trailing_name
src/core/graph/graph.mbt::value:rewrite_.doc:
Resolve an edge endpoint against known symbols and modules by matching on
 the endpoint's trailing id segment. This covers the cross-spec mismatch
 where semantics scripts emit `<file>::<name>` while `attrs def` registers
 `<file>::<ns>:<name>`, or where nested `<parent>.<name>` references need
 to be rewritten to the canonical symbol id.

src/core/graph/graph.mbt::value:resolve_.name: resolve_edge_target
src/core/graph/graph.mbt::value:resolve_.doc:
Resolve an edge target against known symbols and modules.
 If the target already exists, returns it unchanged. If a `declares` edge
 references a target that matches an existing symbol by `(module_id, name)`,
 rewrites it to the canonical symbol ID. Returns the pair
 `(resolved_id, was_rewritten)`; `was_rewritten` is true only when the ID
 changed.

src/core/graph/graph.mbt::value:to_json_.name: to_json_string
src/core/graph/graph.mbt::value:to_json_.doc:
Convert CodeGraph to JSON string following KGF format.
 Format:
 {
   "modules": { "id": { "file": "..." } },
   "symbols": { "id": { "name": "...", "kind": "...", "ns": "...", "module": "..." } },
   "edges": [{ "kind": "...", "from": "...", "to": "...", ...attrs }]
 }

 Before writing, edge endpoints are reconciled against the node tables:
 - `declares` edges whose `to` does not match a symbol ID are remapped to the
   canonical symbol by `(from, name)` lookup (KGF scripts often emit
   `<file>::<name>` while `attrs def` registers `<file>::<ns>:<name>`).
 - Any `to` that still has no matching node is registered as an external
   module with `file: null` so consumers see every endpoint in `modules` or
   `symbols`.

src/core/graph/graph.mbt::value:from_jso.doc: Parse JSON string to CodeGraph following KGF format.

src/config/reconcile_config.mbt::value:p.name: parse_shared_toml_sections
src/config/reconcile_config.mbt::value:p.doc:
Dispatch a single TOML key-value pair to the shared reconcile/digest section
 handlers. Both project and global TOML parsers carry these identical sections,
 so this helper centralises the dispatch to avoid duplication.
 Returns the updated `(ReconcileFileConfig, DigestFileConfig)` pair.

src/segmentation/types/types.mbt::value:.doc:
Common interface for segmentation strategies.
 Each strategy implements this trait to provide a consistent API.

src/segmentation/types/types.mbt::value:.name: WindowDivergence
src/segmentation/types/types.mbt::value:.doc:
Result of window divergence calculation.
 Shared by tfidf and window segmentation strategies.

src/segmentation/utils/compression_dista.name: char_to_utf8
src/segmentation/utils/compression_dista.doc: Convert a character to UTF-8 bytes and append to buffer.

src/segmentation/utils/compression_dista.name: trigram_hash
src/segmentation/utils/compression_dista.doc: Hash a 3-byte trigram into a table index.

src/segmentation/utils/compression_dista.name: compress
src/segmentation/utils/compression_dista.doc:
Compress text using LZ77-style algorithm with hash chain acceleration.
 Uses an 8KB sliding window with trigram hash chains to find matches efficiently.
 The output format:
 - Literal byte: 0x00 followed by the byte
 - Back reference: 0x01 followed by 2-byte offset and 1-byte length

src/segmentation/utils/compression_dista.name: compressed_size
src/segmentation/utils/compression_dista.doc: Get the compressed size of text in bytes.

src/segmentation/utils/compression_dista.name: ncd
src/segmentation/utils/compression_dista.doc:
Calculate Normalized Compression Distance between two texts.
 NCD(x, y) = (C(xy) - min(C(x), C(y))) / max(C(x), C(y))

 Uses LZ77-style compression to measure text similarity.
 Similar texts compress better together (lower NCD), while different texts
 do not benefit from concatenation (higher NCD).

 Returns NCD value between 0 (identical) and ~1 (completely different).

src/segmentation/utils/compression_dista.name: calculate_adjacent_ncd
src/segmentation/utils/compression_dista.doc:
Calculate NCD values for sliding window of adjacent texts.
 For texts [a, b, c, d], calculates:
 - ncd(a, b)
 - ncd(b, c)
 - ncd(c, d)

 Returns array of NCD values (length = texts.length - 1).

src/segmentation/utils/compression_dista.name: min_max_normalize
src/segmentation/utils/compression_dista.doc:
Normalize an array of values using Min-Max normalization.
 Returns values in range [0, 1].
 If all values are the same, returns 0.5 for all.

 WARNING: Min-Max is sensitive to outliers. Prefer `robust_normalize`
 for signals that may contain extreme values.

src/segmentation/utils/compression_dista.name: robust_normalize
src/segmentation/utils/compression_dista.doc:
Normalize an array of values using robust z-score (median + MAD).
 Resistant to outliers unlike Min-Max normalization.
 Output is shifted to [0, 1] range via sigmoid-like clamping.
 If MAD is zero (all values equal or nearly so), returns 0.5 for all.

src/segmentation/utils/compression_dista.name: reciprocal_rank_fusion
src/segmentation/utils/compression_dista.doc:
Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) for combining multiple ranked signal arrays.
 Each input array is ranked independently, then fused via:
   score(i) = sum_j( 1 / (k + rank_j(i)) )
 where k is a smoothing constant (typically 60).
 Returns fused scores normalized to [0, 1].

 All input arrays must have the same length. Higher values = higher rank.

src/segmentation/utils/sentence_boundary.name: is_sentence_terminator
src/segmentation/utils/sentence_boundary.doc:

src/segmentation/utils/sentence_boundary.name: is_trailing_punctuation

src/segmentation/utils/sentence_boundary.doc:
Update the quote stack state for one character.
 Opening quote => push to stack.
 Matching closing quote => pop from stack.

src/segmentation/utils/sentence_boundary.doc: Build quote stack state up to end_pos (exclusive).

src/segmentation/utils/sentence_boundary.doc: Check if currently inside a protected quote (under max length).

Find sentence boundary positions in text.

src/segmentation/utils/sentence_boundary.name: find_next_boundary
src/segmentation/utils/sentence_boundary.doc:
Find the next sentence boundary from a given position.
 Returns end position of the next sentence, or text.length() if none found.

src/segmentation/utils/sentence_boundary.name: find_next_boundary_default
src/segmentation/utils/sentence_boundary.doc: Find the next sentence boundary with default options.

src/segmentation/utils/sentence_boundary.name: is_at_boundary
src/segmentation/utils/sentence_boundary.doc:
Check if a position is at a sentence boundary.
 Returns true if the position is immediately after trailing punctuation.

src/segmentation/utils/types.mbt::value:.doc: Default maximum length protected from punctuation splitting inside Japanese quotes.

src/segmentation/utils/common.mbt::value.name: is_whitespace_only
src/segmentation/utils/common.mbt::value.doc: Check if a string contains only whitespace.

src/segmentation/utils/common.mbt::value.name: smooth_moving_average
src/segmentation/utils/common.mbt::value.doc:
Smooth an array of values using a symmetric moving average.
 The window extends `radius` elements on each side (total width = 2*radius + 1).
 Edges use partial windows (naturally shrink).

src/segmentation/utils/common.mbt::value.name: calculate_prominence
src/segmentation/utils/common.mbt::value.doc:
Calculate peak prominence for each index.
 Prominence measures how much a peak stands out from its surrounding valleys.
 For each point, scan left and right until a higher value is found;
 the minimum encountered on each side is the "key col" (valley).
 Prominence = value - max(left_col, right_col).
 For boundary peaks (no higher peak on one side), only the other side counts.

src/segmentation/utils/common.mbt::value.name: find_local_maxima
src/segmentation/utils/common.mbt::value.doc:
Find local maxima indices where values exceed the threshold.
 Uses moving average smoothing (radius=1) to reduce noise, then filters
 by peak prominence to reject insignificant local bumps.
 A peak must satisfy:
   1. Smoothed value >= threshold
   2. Is a local maximum in the smoothed signal
   3. Peak prominence >= 20% of the signal range (adaptive)

src/segmentation/sentence/splitter.mbt::.name: default_quote_safe_max_length
src/segmentation/sentence/splitter.mbt::.doc:
Default maximum length protected from punctuation splitting inside quotes.
 If quoted content exceeds this length, punctuation-based boundaries are allowed.

src/segmentation/sentence/splitter.mbt::.doc:
Check if a character is an opening quote (Japanese only for reliable protection).
 Note: English ASCII quotes (" and ') are ambiguous (same char for open/close),
 so we only protect Japanese quotes for reliable behavior.

src/segmentation/sentence/splitter.mbt::.doc: Get the matching opening quote for a closing quote.

src/segmentation/sentence/splitter.mbt::.name: is_inside_protected_quote
src/segmentation/sentence/splitter.mbt::.doc: Check if currently inside a protected quote (under max length).

src/segmentation/sentence/splitter.mbt::.name: split_sentences
src/segmentation/sentence/splitter.mbt::.doc:
Split text into sentences with default options (quote protection enabled).

 Handles:
 - English sentence endings: `.` `!` `?`
 - Japanese sentence endings: `。` `！` `？` `．`
 - Newlines as sentence breaks
 - Consecutive punctuation marks
 - Quote protection: punctuation inside quotes does not split sentences

src/segmentation/sentence/splitter.mbt::.name: split_sentences_with_options
src/segmentation/sentence/splitter.mbt::.doc:
Split text into sentences with custom options.

 Handles:
 - English sentence endings: `.` `!` `?`
 - Japanese sentence endings: `。` `！` `？` `．`
 - Newlines as sentence breaks
 - Consecutive punctuation marks
 - Quote protection (configurable): punctuation inside quotes does not split sentences
