src/digest/config/config.mbt::value:vect.doc: Path to the vector database for a given provider key.

src/digest/config/config.mbt::value:voca.name: vocabulary_json_path
src/digest/config/config.mbt::value:voca.doc: Path to the vocabulary file for a given provider key (TF-IDF only).

src/digest/config/config.mbt::value:comp.name: compute_index_file_names
src/digest/config/config.mbt::value:comp.doc: Compute the list of all index file names for a given set of provider keys.

src/digest/manifest/manifest.mbt::value:.name: FileEntry
src/digest/manifest/manifest.mbt::value:.doc: Entry for a single file in the manifest.

src/digest/manifest/manifest.mbt::value:.name: FunctionEntry
src/digest/manifest/manifest.mbt::value:.doc: Entry for a single function in the manifest.

src/digest/manifest/manifest.mbt::value:.name: ProviderMeta
src/digest/manifest/manifest.mbt::value:.doc: Per-provider metadata stored in the manifest.

src/digest/manifest/manifest.mbt::value:.name: allocate_vector_id
src/digest/manifest/manifest.mbt::value:.doc: Allocate a new vector ID for a given provider.

src/digest/manifest/manifest.mbt::value:.name: update_function
src/digest/manifest/manifest.mbt::value:.doc: Update or add a function entry.

src/digest/manifest/manifest.mbt::value:.name: get_deleted_functions
src/digest/manifest/manifest.mbt::value:.doc: Find deleted functions (in manifest but not in current units).

src/digest/manifest/manifest.mbt::value:.name: remove_deleted_functions
src/digest/manifest/manifest.mbt::value:.doc:
Remove deleted functions and return their vector IDs for cleanup.
 Returns (updated manifest, provider_key -> removed vector IDs).

src/digest/manifest/manifest.mbt::value:.name: from_json_string
src/digest/manifest/manifest.mbt::value:.doc:
Parse manifest from JSON string.
 Returns None if the JSON is invalid or the manifest version is incompatible,
 which causes the caller to trigger a full rebuild.

src/digest/index/native.mbt::value:acqui.name: acquire_build_lock
src/digest/index/native.mbt::value:acqui.doc: Acquire a build lock for the given index directory.

src/digest/index/native.mbt::value:relea.name: release_build_lock
src/digest/index/native.mbt::value:relea.doc: Release the build lock for the given index directory.

src/digest/index/native.mbt::value:delet.name: delete_index_files
src/digest/index/native.mbt::value:delet.doc: Delete all index files in the given directory (for full rebuild).

src/digest/index/index.mbt::value:Digest.name: DigestIndex
src/digest/index/index.mbt::value:Digest.doc:
DigestIndex - Main index structure.
 N-provider: engines and reverse indexes are keyed by provider key.

src/digest/index/index.mbt::value:has_pr.name: has_provider
src/digest/index/index.mbt::value:has_pr.doc: Whether this index has a given provider.

src/digest/index/index.mbt::value:first_.name: first_provider_of_kind
src/digest/index/index.mbt::value:first_.doc: Find the first provider key of a given kind.

src/digest/index/index.mbt::value:serial.name: serialize
src/digest/index/index.mbt::value:serial.doc:
Serialize index for persistence.
 Returns (manifest_json, provider_key -> vector_db_bytes).

src/digest/traverse/traverse.mbt::value:.name: at_depth
src/digest/traverse/traverse.mbt::value:.doc: Get functions at a specific depth.

src/digest/extract/extract.mbt::value:de.name: default
src/digest/extract/extract.mbt::value:de.doc:
Create default extraction config.
 Excludes Param (function parameters) by default.

src/digest/extract/extract.mbt::value:al.name: all
src/digest/extract/extract.mbt::value:al.doc: Create config that includes all symbols (no exclusions).

src/digest/extract/extract.mbt::value:sh.name: should_include_kind
src/digest/extract/extract.mbt::value:sh.doc: Check if a symbol kind should be included (not in exclude list).

src/platform/platform.mbt::value:detect_.name: detect_platform
src/platform/platform.mbt::value:detect_.doc: Detect the current operating system platform.

src/platform/platform.mbt::value:detect_.name: detect_arch
src/platform/platform.mbt::value:detect_.doc:
Detect the CPU architecture.
 Windows: PROCESSOR_ARCHITECTURE is authoritative.
 Unix: HOSTTYPE env var, then probes /usr/lib/aarch64-linux-gnu (Debian/Ubuntu arm64
 marker) and /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu (x64 marker) as a filesystem heuristic.

src/embedding/config/config.mbt::value:n.name: new
src/embedding/config/config.mbt::value:n.doc: Create EmbeddingApiConfig with defaults for OpenAI.

src/embedding/config/config.mbt::value:E.name: EmbeddingProvider
src/embedding/config/config.mbt::value:E.doc:
Embedding provider type.
 Used for CLI parsing and display. The actual config uses providers array.

src/embedding/config/config.mbt::value:r.name: resolve_provider
src/embedding/config/config.mbt::value:r.doc:
Resolve an EmbeddingProvider from named CLI/config parameters.

 provider_name: "openai" | "tfidf" | "auto" | ""
 api_key_env:   environment variable name for the API key
 base_url:      API base URL
 model:         embedding model name
 dim:           embedding dimension

 Rules:
 - "tfidf": always returns TfIdf(dim).
 - "openai": requires the env var to be set; returns Err if missing.
 - "auto" / "": uses OpenAI if the env var is set, TfIdf otherwise.
 - Unknown name: returns Err.

src/embedding/config/config.mbt::value:f.name: from_json
src/embedding/config/config.mbt::value:f.doc:
Deserialize EmbeddingProvider from JSON.
 Returns TfIdf(DEFAULT_TFIDF_DIM) if the JSON is unrecognized.

src/embedding/config/config.mbt::value:V.name: VcdbStrategy
src/embedding/config/config.mbt::value:V.doc: vcdb indexing strategy (Single Source of Truth).

src/embedding/config/config.mbt::value:P.name: ProviderConfig
src/embedding/config/config.mbt::value:P.doc:
Per-provider configuration.
 Each provider is identified by a unique key and has a kind ("tfidf" or "remote").

src/embedding/config/config.mbt::value:r.name: remote
src/embedding/config/config.mbt::value:r.doc:
Create a remote provider config from an EmbeddingApiConfig.
 Key defaults to the sanitized model name.

src/embedding/config/config.mbt::value:s.name: sanitize_provider_key
src/embedding/config/config.mbt::value:s.doc:
Sanitize a string for use as a provider key (filesystem-safe).
 Replaces non-alphanumeric chars (except hyphen, underscore, dot) with hyphen.

src/spec/draft/analysis.mbt::value:rende.name: render_markdown
src/spec/draft/analysis.mbt::value:rende.doc:
Render requirements as cc-sdd compatible markdown.

 Top-level sections (level ≤ 2) become `### Requirement N: Title`.
 Sub-sections (level ≥ 3) become `#### N.M: Title` under the preceding
 top-level requirement, producing the `N.M` IDs that cc-sdd design/tasks
 phases expect.

src/spec/align/spec_adapter.mbt::value:t.name: tokenize_for_scoring
src/spec/align/spec_adapter.mbt::value:t.doc: Tokenize text into lowercase words for classification scoring.

src/spec/align/spec_adapter.mbt::value:c.name: classify_normative_strength
src/spec/align/spec_adapter.mbt::value:c.doc:
Classify the normative strength of a section body.
 Returns "SHALL", "MUST", "SHOULD", or None.
 This detects explicit normative keywords in the body text,
 independent of any trained classification model.

src/spec/align/spec_adapter.mbt::value:s.name: should_include_role
src/spec/align/spec_adapter.mbt::value:s.doc:
Determines whether a given role/class label indicates a requirement
 section that should be included.

 This is a blacklist: known non-requirement roles are excluded.
 Unknown roles and empty strings are included.
 This logic is shared between document_section_role (from semantics)
 and classify model predictions (from `=== classify`), unifying the
 two classification paths under the same inclusion semantics.

src/spec/align/analysis.mbt::value:extra.name: extract_inline_code_refs
src/spec/align/analysis.mbt::value:extra.doc:
Extract backtick-enclosed identifiers from criteria text.
 E.g. "include a `detail` message" → ["detail"]

src/spec/align/analysis.mbt::value:ident.name: identifier_match_score
src/spec/align/analysis.mbt::value:ident.doc:
Score based on direct identifier matching between spec inline code refs
 and implementation symbol name. Returns a high score (0.5) if the
 implementation name contains any of the spec identifiers.

src/spec/align/analysis.mbt::value:extra.name: extract_alnum
src/spec/align/analysis.mbt::value:extra.doc:
Extract word characters from a string, stripping punctuation and symbols.
 Retains: letters (any script), digits, underscore, and spaces.
 Used to normalize spec identifiers (e.g. `data:` → `data`) for
 fuzzy matching against code.

src/spec/align/analysis.mbt::value:has_f.name: has_function_in_same_file
src/spec/align/analysis.mbt::value:has_f.doc:
Check whether any function declaration exists in the same file.
 Used to distinguish type-only stub files from files that also contain
 function implementations. Note: KGF uses SpanPreference::Shortest for
 functions, so end_line == start_line — we cannot check body length here.
 The presence of any function declaration is sufficient evidence that the
 file is not type-only.

src/spec/align/analysis.mbt::value:file_.name: file_hashes_match
src/spec/align/analysis.mbt::value:file_.doc: Check whether two file hash maps are identical (same keys and values).

src/similarity/tfidf_strategy/tfidf.mbt:.name: TfidfStrategy
src/similarity/tfidf_strategy/tfidf.mbt:.doc:
TF-IDF based similarity strategy.
 For efficient batch processing, use TfidfBatch::from_tokens instead.

src/similarity/tfidf_strategy/tfidf.mbt:.name: TfidfBatch
src/similarity/tfidf_strategy/tfidf.mbt:.doc:
Batch TF-IDF similarity calculator.
 Precomputes TF-IDF vectors for efficient pairwise comparison.

src/similarity/tfidf_strategy/tfidf.mbt:.name: from_tokens
src/similarity/tfidf_strategy/tfidf.mbt:.doc: Create a batch calculator from pre-tokenized documents.

src/similarity/tfidf_strategy/tfidf.mbt:.name: length
src/similarity/tfidf_strategy/tfidf.mbt:.doc: Get the number of documents in the batch.

src/similarity/tfidf_strategy/tfidf.mbt:.name: similarity
src/similarity/tfidf_strategy/tfidf.mbt:.doc: Calculate similarity between two documents by index.

src/similarity/tfidf_strategy/tfidf.mbt:.name: all_pairs_above_threshold
src/similarity/tfidf_strategy/tfidf.mbt:.doc:
Calculate all pairwise similarities above a threshold.
 Returns array of (i, j, similarity) where i < j.

src/similarity/apted/algorithm.mbt::valu.name: compute_tree_edit_distance_indexed
src/similarity/apted/algorithm.mbt::valu.doc: Compute tree edit distance on pre-indexed trees.

src/similarity/apted/algorithm.mbt::valu.name: normalized_tree_edit_distance_indexed
src/similarity/apted/algorithm.mbt::valu.doc:
Normalized tree edit distance on pre-indexed trees.
 Returns a value in [0, 1] where 0 = identical trees.

src/similarity/apted/algorithm.mbt::valu.name: tree_similarity_indexed
src/similarity/apted/algorithm.mbt::valu.doc:
Tree similarity on pre-indexed trees.
 Returns a value in [0, 1] where 1 = identical trees.

src/similarity/apted/algorithm.mbt::valu.name: unit_cost_tree_similarity_upper_bound
src/similarity/apted/algorithm.mbt::valu.doc:
Exact upper bound on unit-cost tree_similarity based on sizes alone.

 With CostConfig::default() (all costs = 1.0):
   raw_dist >= |size1 - size2|
   max_dist = size1 + size2
   similarity <= 1 - |size1-size2| / (size1+size2)

src/similarity/apted/algorithm.mbt::valu.name: compute_tree_edit_distance
src/similarity/apted/algorithm.mbt::valu.doc:
Compute tree edit distance between two trees using Zhang-Shasha algorithm.
 Returns the minimum cost to transform tree1 into tree2.

src/similarity/apted/algorithm.mbt::valu.name: compute_forest_dist
src/similarity/apted/algorithm.mbt::valu.doc:
Compute forest distances for a pair of keyroots.
 This fills in tree_dist entries for all nodes in the subtrees rooted at i and j.

src/similarity/apted/algorithm.mbt::valu.name: normalized_tree_edit_distance
src/similarity/apted/algorithm.mbt::valu.doc:
Compute normalized tree edit distance.
 Returns a value in [0, 1] where 0 = identical trees.

src/similarity/apted/algorithm.mbt::valu.name: tree_similarity
src/similarity/apted/algorithm.mbt::valu.doc:
Compute tree similarity (1 - normalized distance).
 Returns a value in [0, 1] where 1 = identical trees.

src/similarity/apted/tsed.mbt::value:pre.name: prepare_tsed_tree
src/similarity/apted/tsed.mbt::value:pre.doc:
Prepare a tree for TSED: apply label finalization and index it.
 Returns the indexed tree and its size for reuse.

src/similarity/apted/tsed.mbt::value:cal.name: calculate_tsed_indexed
src/similarity/apted/tsed.mbt::value:cal.doc:
Calculate TSED similarity on pre-indexed trees.
 Returns a value in [0, 1] where 1 = identical trees.

src/similarity/apted/tsed.mbt::value:tse.name: tsed_similarity_upper_bound
src/similarity/apted/tsed.mbt::value:tse.doc:
Exact upper bound on TSED similarity based on sizes alone.

 With max-based normalization:
   best case dist = max_size - min_size
   upper_bound = 1 - (max_size - min_size) / max_size = min_size / max_size

src/similarity/apted/tsed.mbt::value:cal.name: calculate_tsed
src/similarity/apted/tsed.mbt::value:cal.doc:
Calculate TSED similarity between two trees.
 Returns a value in [0, 1] where 1 = identical trees.

 Normalization: max(1 - dist / max(size1, size2), 0)

 This normalization ensures:
 - Identical trees → 1.0 (regardless of size)
 - All labels different, same structure → 0.0
 - No magic number penalties

src/similarity/apted/tsed.mbt::value:cal.name: calculate_tsed_distance
src/similarity/apted/tsed.mbt::value:cal.doc:
Calculate TSED distance between two trees.
 Returns 1 - similarity, a value in [0, 1] where 0 = identical trees.

src/similarity/apted/tsed.mbt::value:sho.name: should_compare_trees
src/similarity/apted/tsed.mbt::value:sho.doc:
Early pruning: determine if two trees are worth comparing.
 Uses size ratio to estimate an upper bound on similarity.
 Returns true if comparison should proceed.

src/similarity/apted/peg_adapter.mbt::va.name: peg_cost_config
src/similarity/apted/peg_adapter.mbt::va.doc:
Cost configuration optimized for PEG AST comparison.
 Uses lower cost for renaming between similar node types.

src/similarity/apted/peg_adapter.mbt::va.name: peg_tsed_config
src/similarity/apted/peg_adapter.mbt::va.doc: Default TSED configuration for PEG AST comparison.

src/similarity/apted/types.mbt::value:Tr.name: TreeNode
src/similarity/apted/types.mbt::value:Tr.doc: Generic tree node representation for tree edit distance computation.

src/similarity/apted/types.mbt::value:le.name: leaf
src/similarity/apted/types.mbt::value:le.doc: Create a leaf node with no children.

src/similarity/apted/types.mbt::value:is.name: is_leaf
src/similarity/apted/types.mbt::value:is.doc: Check if this is a leaf node.

src/similarity/apted/types.mbt::value:si.name: size
src/similarity/apted/types.mbt::value:si.doc: Calculate the size of the tree (total number of nodes).

src/similarity/apted/types.mbt::value:he.name: height
src/similarity/apted/types.mbt::value:he.doc: Calculate the height of the tree.

src/similarity/apted/types.mbt::value:No.name: NodeInfo
src/similarity/apted/types.mbt::value:No.doc:
Node information for APTED algorithm indexing.
 Computed during tree preprocessing.

src/similarity/apted/types.mbt::value:Co.name: CostConfig
src/similarity/apted/types.mbt::value:Co.doc: Cost configuration for tree edit operations.

src/similarity/apted/types.mbt::value:de.name: default
src/similarity/apted/types.mbt::value:de.doc: Create default cost config (all operations cost 1.0).

src/similarity/apted/types.mbt::value:la.name: label_cost
src/similarity/apted/types.mbt::value:la.doc: Calculate the cost to transform one label to another.

src/similarity/apted/types.mbt::value:TS.name: TSEDConfig
src/similarity/apted/types.mbt::value:TS.doc: Configuration for TSED (Tree Structure Edit Distance) calculation.

src/similarity/apted/types.mbt::value:wi.name: with_finalized_labels
src/similarity/apted/types.mbt::value:wi.doc:
Create a new tree with finalized labels.
 Non-leaf nodes get child count appended: "Seq" → "Seq_3" (if 3 children).
 Leaf nodes are unchanged. The original tree is not modified.

src/similarity/apted/node_info.mbt::valu.name: IndexedTree
src/similarity/apted/node_info.mbt::valu.doc:
Indexed node information for Zhang-Shasha algorithm.
 Uses postorder numbering (1-indexed for algorithm convenience).

src/similarity/apted/node_info.mbt::valu.name: from_tree_with_size
src/similarity/apted/node_info.mbt::valu.doc: Create an IndexedTree from a TreeNode when size is already known.

src/similarity/apted/node_info.mbt::valu.name: from_tree
src/similarity/apted/node_info.mbt::valu.doc: Create an IndexedTree from a TreeNode.

src/similarity/apted/node_info.mbt::valu.name: index_tree_with_size
src/similarity/apted/node_info.mbt::valu.doc: Index a tree and return both the indexed tree and its size.

src/similarity/apted/node_info.mbt::valu.name: fill_postorder
src/similarity/apted/node_info.mbt::valu.doc:
Fill arrays using postorder traversal.
 Returns the postorder number of this node.

src/similarity/apted/node_info.mbt::valu.name: sort_keyroots_by_postorder
src/similarity/apted/node_info.mbt::valu.doc: Sort keyroots by their postorder number (ascending).

src/similarity/apted/node_info.mbt::valu.name: get_label
src/similarity/apted/node_info.mbt::valu.doc: Get label at postorder index i (1-indexed).

src/similarity/apted/node_info.mbt::valu.name: get_l
src/similarity/apted/node_info.mbt::valu.doc: Get leftmost leaf at postorder index i (1-indexed).

src/similarity/types/types.mbt::value:Si.name: SimilarityResult
src/similarity/types/types.mbt::value:Si.doc: Result of similarity calculation between two items.

src/similarity/types/types.mbt::value:Si.name: SimilarityStrategy
src/similarity/types/types.mbt::value:Si.doc:
Common interface for text similarity strategies.
 Each strategy implements this trait to provide a consistent API.

src/similarity/types/types.mbt::value:Hy.name: HybridConfig
src/similarity/types/types.mbt::value:Hy.doc: Configuration for the hybrid NCD + TF-IDF strategy.

src/similarity/types/types.mbt::value:ne.name: new
src/similarity/types/types.mbt::value:ne.doc: Create a default HybridConfig with equal weights (0.5, 0.5).

src/similarity/types/types.mbt::value:cr.name: create
src/similarity/types/types.mbt::value:cr.doc:
Create a HybridConfig with custom weights.
 Weights are normalized to sum to 1.0.

src/similarity/ncd/ncd.mbt::value:NCDStr.name: NCDStrategy
src/similarity/ncd/ncd.mbt::value:NCDStr.doc:
NCD-based similarity strategy.
 Uses compression-based distance to measure semantic similarity.

src/similarity/ncd/ncd.mbt::value:calcul.name: calculate_adjacent_ncd
src/similarity/ncd/ncd.mbt::value:calcul.doc:
Calculate NCD distances for adjacent texts in an array.
 Returns array where result[i] = ncd(texts[i], texts[i+1]).

src/similarity/strategy.mbt::value:Strat.name: StrategyKind
src/similarity/strategy.mbt::value:Strat.doc: Strategy kind enum for unified handling.

src/similarity/strategy.mbt::value:parse.name: parse_with_weights
src/similarity/strategy.mbt::value:parse.doc: Parse strategy with optional hybrid weights.

src/similarity/strategy.mbt::value:is_te.name: is_text_strategy
src/similarity/strategy.mbt::value:is_te.doc: Check if strategy operates on text (vs. trees).

src/similarity/strategy.mbt::value:is_tr.name: is_tree_strategy
src/similarity/strategy.mbt::value:is_tr.doc: Check if strategy operates on trees.

src/similarity/strategy.mbt::value:text_.name: text_similarity
src/similarity/strategy.mbt::value:text_.doc:
Calculate text similarity using the strategy.
 Returns None for tree strategies.

src/similarity/strategy.mbt::value:calcu.name: calculate
src/similarity/strategy.mbt::value:calcu.doc:
Calculate similarity with automatic dispatch based on input type.
 For text strategies, compares strings directly.
 For tree strategies, parses bracket notation into trees.

src/similarity/strategy.mbt::value:parse.name: parse_bracket_tree
src/similarity/strategy.mbt::value:parse.doc:
Parse bracket notation into TreeNode.
 Format: "Label" or "Label(Child1,Child2,...)"

src/similarity/strategy.mbt::value:parse.name: parse_tree_recursive
src/similarity/strategy.mbt::value:parse.doc: Recursive tree parser for bracket notation.

src/similarity/hybrid/hybrid.mbt::value:.name: HybridStrategy
src/similarity/hybrid/hybrid.mbt::value:.doc:
Hybrid similarity strategy combining NCD and TF-IDF.
 Uses weighted average of both metrics for robust similarity measurement.

src/similarity/hybrid/hybrid.mbt::value:.name: new
src/similarity/hybrid/hybrid.mbt::value:.doc: Create a new HybridStrategy with default config (50/50 weights).

src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:InputSche.name: InputSchema
src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:InputSche.doc: JSON Schema for a tool's input parameters.

src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:PropertyS.name: PropertySchema
src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:PropertyS.doc: Schema for a single property in a tool's input.

src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:ToolDefin.name: ToolDefinition
src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:ToolDefin.doc: An MCP tool definition exposed by this server.

src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:string_pr.name: string_prop
src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:string_pr.doc: Create a simple string property schema.

src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:enum_prop.name: enum_prop
src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:enum_prop.doc: Create a string property schema with allowed values.

src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:string_ar.name: string_array_prop
src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:string_ar.doc: Create a string array property schema.

src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:TextConte.name: TextContent
src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:TextConte.doc: MCP content block — text content only (sufficient for CLI tool results).

src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:call_resu.name: call_result
src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:call_resu.doc: Build a tools/call result JSON from content blocks.

src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:text_resu.name: text_result
src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:text_resu.doc: Shorthand: success result with a single text block.

src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:error_res.name: error_result
src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:error_res.doc: Shorthand: error result with a single text block.

src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:ServerInf.name: ServerInfo
src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:ServerInf.doc: MCP server info for the initialize response.

src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:server_ca.name: server_capabilities
src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:server_ca.doc:
Build the capabilities object for the initialize response.
 Currently only tools capability is advertised.

src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:initializ.name: initialize_result
src/mcp/types/types.mbt::value:initializ.doc: Build a complete MCP initialize result.

src/mcp/server/server.mbt::value:ToolHan.name: ToolHandler
src/mcp/server/server.mbt::value:ToolHan.doc:
A registered tool handler. Takes JSON params, returns a result.
 Async to support handlers that call async commands (e.g. reconcile).

src/mcp/server/server.mbt::value:McpServ.name: McpServer
src/mcp/server/server.mbt::value:McpServ.doc: The MCP server. Holds registered tools and dispatches requests.

src/mcp/protocol/jsonrpc.mbt::value:from.name: from_json_string
src/mcp/protocol/jsonrpc.mbt::value:from.doc:
Parse a JSON string into a JSON-RPC 2.0 request.
 Returns None if the JSON is malformed or missing required fields.

src/mcp/protocol/jsonrpc.mbt::value:from.name: from_json
src/mcp/protocol/jsonrpc.mbt::value:from.doc: Parse a Json value into a JSON-RPC 2.0 request.

src/mcp/protocol/jsonrpc.mbt::value:is_n.name: is_notification
src/mcp/protocol/jsonrpc.mbt::value:is_n.doc: Check whether this request is a notification (no id field).

src/ignorefile/load.mbt::value:load_from.name: load_from_dir
src/ignorefile/load.mbt::value:load_from.doc:
Load and parse a gitignore-syntax file from a directory.
 SoT for all ignore file reading.

src/ignorefile/parse.mbt::value:parse.name: parse

src/docgen/analyze/types.mbt::value:get_.doc: Return the documentation comment, if any.

src/docgen/analyze/types.mbt::value:get_.name: get_parent
src/docgen/analyze/types.mbt::value:get_.doc: Return the parent symbol ID, if any.

src/docgen/analyze/types.mbt::value:get_.name: get_file
src/docgen/analyze/types.mbt::value:get_.doc: Return the file where this symbol is declared.

src/docgen/analyze/types.mbt::value:Anal.name: AnalysisResult
src/docgen/analyze/types.mbt::value:Anal.doc: Result of the complete analysis pipeline.

src/docgen/analyze/types.mbt::value:add_.name: add_file
src/docgen/analyze/types.mbt::value:add_.doc: Add an analyzed file to the result.

src/docgen/analyze/types.mbt::value:add_.name: add_symbol
src/docgen/analyze/types.mbt::value:add_.doc: Add an extracted documentation symbol to the result.

src/docgen/analyze/types.mbt::value:set_.name: set_module_doc
src/docgen/analyze/types.mbt::value:set_.doc: Set module-level documentation for a given module path.

src/docgen/query/types.mbt::value:Module.name: ModuleDep
src/docgen/query/types.mbt::value:Module.doc: Module dependency information extracted from ModuleDependsOn edges.

src/docgen/query/types.mbt::value:CallIn.name: CallInfo
src/docgen/query/types.mbt::value:CallIn.doc: Call relationship extracted from Calls edges.

src/docgen/query/types.mbt::value:TypeRe.name: TypeRelation
src/docgen/query/types.mbt::value:TypeRe.doc: Type hierarchy relationship extracted from Extends/Implements edges.

src/docgen/query/types.mbt::value:Symbol.name: SymbolRef
src/docgen/query/types.mbt::value:Symbol.doc: Symbol reference extracted from References edges.

src/docgen/query/types.mbt::value:get_de.name: get_dep_kind
src/docgen/query/types.mbt::value:get_de.doc: Get the dependency kind (import, require, etc).

src/docgen/query/types.mbt::value:get_fi.name: get_file
src/docgen/query/types.mbt::value:get_fi.doc: Get the file where the call occurs.

src/docgen/query/types.mbt::value:get_re.name: get_ref_site
src/docgen/query/types.mbt::value:get_re.doc: Get the reference site (where the symbol is used).

src/docgen/query/types.mbt::value:get_re.name: get_ref_kind
src/docgen/query/types.mbt::value:get_re.doc: Get the reference kind (call, access, etc).

src/docgen/query/types.mbt::value:get_ki.name: get_kind
src/docgen/query/types.mbt::value:get_ki.doc: Get the symbol kind (Function, Class, etc).

src/docgen/query/types.mbt::value:Circul.name: CircularDep
src/docgen/query/types.mbt::value:Circul.doc: Circular dependency information extracted from CircularDependency edges.

src/glob/glob.mbt::value:glob_match.name: glob_match
src/glob/glob.mbt::value:glob_match.doc:
Simple glob pattern matching.
 Supports:
 - * matches any sequence of characters (except /)
 - ** matches any sequence including /
 - ? matches single character
 - [abc] matches any character in set
 - [!abc] matches any character not in set
 - [a-z] matches character range

src/glob/glob.mbt::value:glob_match_anyw.name: glob_match_anywhere
src/glob/glob.mbt::value:glob_match_anyw.doc: Match pattern anywhere in the path (useful for unanchored patterns).

src/glob/glob.mbt::value:has_glob_patter.name: has_glob_pattern
src/glob/glob.mbt::value:has_glob_patter.doc: Check whether a string contains glob metacharacters (*, ?, [).

src/glob/glob.mbt::value:expand_glob.name: expand_glob
src/glob/glob.mbt::value:expand_glob.doc: Expand a glob pattern against the filesystem and return matching paths.

src/text/embed/tfidf_provider.mbt::value.name: TfidfEmbeddingProvider
src/text/embed/tfidf_provider.mbt::value.doc:
TF-IDF based embedding provider.
 Builds a vocabulary from a text corpus, then projects new texts into
 a fixed-dimension dense vector space using TF-IDF weights.

src/text/embed/tfidf_provider.mbt::value.name: new
src/text/embed/tfidf_provider.mbt::value.doc: Create a new TfidfEmbeddingProvider with given dimension.

src/text/embed/tfidf_provider.mbt::value.name: build_vocabulary
src/text/embed/tfidf_provider.mbt::value.doc:
Build vocabulary from a corpus of texts.
 Selects the top `dim` terms by aggregate TF-IDF weight across the corpus,
 which naturally favors discriminative terms (high IDF) over ubiquitous noise.

src/text/embed/tfidf_provider.mbt::value.name: embed
src/text/embed/tfidf_provider.mbt::value.doc:
Generate a dense embedding vector for the given text.
 Returns an L2-normalized vector of length `dim`.

src/text/embed/tfidf_provider.mbt::value.name: has_vocabulary
src/text/embed/tfidf_provider.mbt::value.doc: Check if vocabulary has been built.

src/text/embed/tfidf_provider.mbt::value.name: to_json_string
src/text/embed/tfidf_provider.mbt::value.doc: Serialize vocabulary and IDF to JSON string for persistence.

src/text/embed/tfidf_provider.mbt::value.name: load_from_json_string
src/text/embed/tfidf_provider.mbt::value.doc:
Restore vocabulary and IDF from JSON string.
 Returns true if successfully loaded, false on parse error.

src/text/embed/tfidf_provider.mbt::value.name: cosine_similarity_dense
src/text/embed/tfidf_provider.mbt::value.doc: Compute cosine similarity between two dense vectors.

src/text/tokenizer/tokenizer.mbt::value:.name: is_cjk_char
src/text/tokenizer/tokenizer.mbt::value:.doc: Check if a character is a CJK (Chinese/Japanese/Korean) character.

src/text/tokenizer/tokenizer.mbt::value:.name: is_word_char
src/text/tokenizer/tokenizer.mbt::value:.doc: Check if a character is a letter or digit (for word tokenization).

src/text/tokenizer/tokenizer.mbt::value:.name: to_lower
src/text/tokenizer/tokenizer.mbt::value:.doc: Convert a character to lowercase (ASCII only).

src/text/tokenizer/tokenizer.mbt::value:.name: normalize_text
src/text/tokenizer/tokenizer.mbt::value:.doc:
Normalize text for TF-IDF processing.
 Converts to lowercase and normalizes whitespace.

src/text/tokenizer/tokenizer.mbt::value:.name: extract_word_tokens
src/text/tokenizer/tokenizer.mbt::value:.doc:
Extract word tokens from text.
 Returns tokens of 2 or more characters.

src/text/tokenizer/tokenizer.mbt::value:.name: extract_character_bigrams
src/text/tokenizer/tokenizer.mbt::value:.doc:
Extract character bigrams from text for CJK processing.
 Removes whitespace before generating bigrams.

src/text/tokenizer/tokenizer.mbt::value:.name: tokenize_for_tfidf
src/text/tokenizer/tokenizer.mbt::value:.doc:
Tokenize text for TF-IDF vectorization.
 Combines word tokens and character bigrams for mixed language support.

src/text/minhash/minhash.mbt::value:MinH.name: MinHashSignature
src/text/minhash/minhash.mbt::value:MinH.doc: A MinHash signature: a fixed-length array of minimum hash values.

src/text/minhash/minhash.mbt::value:from.name: from_tokens
src/text/minhash/minhash.mbt::value:from.doc:
Create a MinHash signature from a set of tokens.
 Uses the formula h_i(x) = (a_i * hash(x) + b_i) mod p
 where p is a large prime and (a_i, b_i) are per-hash-function constants.

src/text/minhash/minhash.mbt::value:jacc.name: jaccard
src/text/minhash/minhash.mbt::value:jacc.doc:
Estimate Jaccard similarity from two MinHash signatures.
 Returns the fraction of hash functions that agree (in [0, 1]).

src/text/minhash/minhash.mbt::value:get_.name: get_values
src/text/minhash/minhash.mbt::value:get_.doc: Get the signature values for LSH banding.

src/text/minhash/minhash.mbt::value:fnv1.name: fnv1a_hash
src/text/minhash/minhash.mbt::value:fnv1.doc: FNV-1a hash for strings, producing a non-negative 32-bit hash.

src/text/minhash/minhash.mbt::value:univ.name: universal_hash
src/text/minhash/minhash.mbt::value:univ.doc:
Universal hash function family: h_i(x) = (a_i * x + b_i) mod p
 Uses a Mersenne prime p = 2^31 - 1 for fast modular arithmetic.
 Coefficients are derived deterministically from the hash function index.

src/text/minhash/lsh.mbt::value:LSHIndex.name: LSHIndex
src/text/minhash/lsh.mbt::value:LSHIndex.doc: LSH index for approximate nearest neighbor search.

src/text/minhash/lsh.mbt::value:new.name: new
src/text/minhash/lsh.mbt::value:new.doc:
Create an LSH index configured for a target Jaccard threshold.
 Automatically selects (bands, rows_per_band) to maximize detection
 probability at the given threshold while keeping false positives low.

 The total number of hash values used = bands × rows_per_band ≤ num_hashes.

src/text/minhash/lsh.mbt::value:init_buc.name: init_buckets
src/text/minhash/lsh.mbt::value:init_buc.doc: Initialize empty bucket maps for each band.

src/text/minhash/lsh.mbt::value:insert.name: insert
src/text/minhash/lsh.mbt::value:insert.doc:
Insert a document's MinHash signature into the LSH index.
 Returns the document's index in the index.

src/text/minhash/lsh.mbt::value:candidat.name: candidate_pairs
src/text/minhash/lsh.mbt::value:candidat.doc:
Query for candidate pairs: all (i, j) where i < j that share
 at least one LSH bucket. Returns deduplicated pairs.

src/text/minhash/lsh.mbt::value:candidat.name: candidate_pairs_above_threshold
src/text/minhash/lsh.mbt::value:candidat.doc:
Query for candidate pairs with MinHash Jaccard re-scoring.
 Returns (i, j, estimated_jaccard) tuples where jaccard >= threshold.

src/text/minhash/lsh.mbt::value:length.name: length
src/text/minhash/lsh.mbt::value:length.doc: Get the number of documents in the index.

src/text/minhash/lsh.mbt::value:hash_ban.name: hash_band
src/text/minhash/lsh.mbt::value:hash_ban.doc:
Hash a band (a contiguous slice of the signature) to a single int.
 Uses a simple polynomial hash over the rows in the band.

src/text/minhash/lsh.mbt::value:select_b.name: select_band_params
src/text/minhash/lsh.mbt::value:select_b.doc:
Select (bands, rows_per_band) that best approximate the target threshold.

 The S-curve inflection point is at t ≈ (1/b)^(1/r).
 We search all valid (b, r) factorizations of num_hashes and pick
 the one whose inflection point is closest to the target.

src/text/tfidf/jsd.mbt::value:jensen_sha.name: jensen_shannon_divergence
src/text/tfidf/jsd.mbt::value:jensen_sha.doc:
Calculate Jensen-Shannon Divergence between two token frequency maps.
 Returns a value in [0, 1] where 0 = identical distributions, 1 = maximally different.

 The input maps are raw term counts (not probabilities); they are
 normalized internally to sum to 1.

src/text/tfidf/jsd.mbt::value:jsd_from_t.name: jsd_from_tokens
src/text/tfidf/jsd.mbt::value:jsd_from_t.doc:
Calculate JSD between two token arrays.
 Convenience function that builds term frequencies internally.

src/text/tfidf/jsd.mbt::value:jsd_simila.name: jsd_similarity
src/text/tfidf/jsd.mbt::value:jsd_simila.doc:
Convert JSD (divergence) to similarity score in [0, 1].
 JSD = 0 → similarity = 1 (identical)
 JSD = 1 → similarity = 0 (maximally different)

src/text/tfidf/jsd.mbt::value:JSDBatch.name: JSDBatch
src/text/tfidf/jsd.mbt::value:JSDBatch.doc:
Batch JSD computation.
 Precomputes term frequencies for all documents, then computes
 pairwise JSD efficiently.

src/text/tfidf/jsd.mbt::value:from_token.name: from_tokens
src/text/tfidf/jsd.mbt::value:from_token.doc: Create a JSDBatch from pre-tokenized documents.

src/text/tfidf/jsd.mbt::value:length.name: length
src/text/tfidf/jsd.mbt::value:length.doc: Get the number of documents in the batch.

src/text/tfidf/jsd.mbt::value:similarity.name: similarity
src/text/tfidf/jsd.mbt::value:similarity.doc:
Calculate JSD similarity between two documents by index.
 Returns 1.0 for identical, 0.0 for maximally different.

src/text/tfidf/jsd.mbt::value:all_pairs_.name: all_pairs_above_threshold
src/text/tfidf/jsd.mbt::value:all_pairs_.doc:
Calculate all pairwise JSD similarities above a threshold.
 Returns array of (i, j, similarity) tuples where i < j.

src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:build_te.name: build_term_frequency
src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:build_te.doc:
Build term frequency map from a list of tokens.
 Returns a map from term to count.

src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:build_do.name: build_document_frequency
src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:build_do.doc:
Build document frequency map from multiple tokenized documents.
 Returns a map from term to the number of documents containing that term.

src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:build_tf.name: build_tfidf_vector
src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:build_tf.doc:
Build a TF-IDF vector from tokens, document frequency, and total document count.
 Uses sublinear TF (1 + ln(count)) to address term saturation.
 IDF(term) = log((1 + total_docs) / (1 + df)) + 1

src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:vector_n.name: vector_norm
src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:vector_n.doc: Calculate the L2 norm of a TF-IDF vector.

src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:cosine_s.name: cosine_similarity
src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:cosine_s.doc:
Calculate cosine similarity between two TF-IDF vectors.
 Returns 1.0 for identical vectors, 0.0 for orthogonal vectors.
 If both vectors are empty, returns 1.0.
 If only one vector is empty, returns 0.0.

src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:cosine_d.name: cosine_distance
src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:cosine_d.doc:
Calculate cosine distance between two TF-IDF vectors.
 Returns 0.0 for identical vectors, 1.0 for orthogonal vectors.
 cosine_distance = 1 - cosine_similarity

src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:calculat.name: calculate_tfidf_distance_from_tokens
src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:calculat.doc: Calculate TF-IDF cosine distance between two pre-tokenized documents.

src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:calculat.name: calculate_adjacent_tfidf_distance_from_tokenized
src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:calculat.doc: Calculate TF-IDF cosine distances between adjacent pre-tokenized documents.

src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:TfidfBat.name: TfidfBatch
src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:TfidfBat.doc:
Precomputed TF-IDF vectors for batch similarity calculation.
 This allows efficient pairwise comparison without re-tokenizing.

src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:from_tok.name: from_tokens
src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:from_tok.doc:
Create a TfidfBatch from pre-tokenized documents.
 Each inner array is a list of tokens for one document.

src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:length.name: length
src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:length.doc: Get the number of documents in the batch.

src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:build_st.name: build_stats
src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:build_st.doc: Get aggregate build statistics for the batch.

src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:similari.name: similarity
src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:similari.doc:
Calculate similarity between two documents by index.
 Returns 1.0 for identical, 0.0 for completely different.

src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:all_pair.name: all_pairs_above_threshold_with_stats
src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:all_pair.doc: Calculate all pairwise similarities above a threshold with diagnostics.

src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:all_pair.name: all_pairs_above_threshold
src/text/tfidf/tfidf.mbt::value:all_pair.doc:
Calculate all pairwise similarities above a threshold.
 Returns array of (i, j, similarity) tuples where i < j and similarity >= threshold.

src/text/tfidf/types.mbt::value:TfidfBat.name: TfidfBatchBuildStats
src/text/tfidf/types.mbt::value:TfidfBat.doc: Build-time statistics for a TF-IDF batch.

src/text/tfidf/types.mbt::value:TfidfPai.name: TfidfPairSearchStats
src/text/tfidf/types.mbt::value:TfidfPai.doc: Search statistics for TF-IDF pair generation.

src/text/tfidf/types.mbt::value:TfidfVec.name: TfidfVector
src/text/tfidf/types.mbt::value:TfidfVec.doc: A sparse TF-IDF vector representation using term-weight mapping.

src/text/tfidf/types.mbt::value:from_ter.name: from_terms
src/text/tfidf/types.mbt::value:from_ter.doc: Create a TfidfVector from an existing term map.

src/text/tfidf/types.mbt::value:get.name: get
src/text/tfidf/types.mbt::value:get.doc: Get the weight for a given term. Returns 0.0 if not found.

src/text/tfidf/types.mbt::value:set.name: set
src/text/tfidf/types.mbt::value:set.doc: Set the weight for a given term.

src/text/tfidf/types.mbt::value:length.name: length
src/text/tfidf/types.mbt::value:length.doc: Get the number of terms in the vector.

src/text/tfidf/types.mbt::value:is_empty.name: is_empty
src/text/tfidf/types.mbt::value:is_empty.doc: Check if the vector is empty.

src/text/tfidf/types.mbt::value:iter.name: iter
src/text/tfidf/types.mbt::value:iter.doc: Get an iterator over the terms.

src/text/tfidf/bm25.mbt::value:build_bm2.name: build_bm25_vector
src/text/tfidf/bm25.mbt::value:build_bm2.doc:
Build a BM25-weighted sparse vector for a document.

 Unlike TF-IDF which uses sublinear TF (1 + ln(count)),
 BM25 uses saturating TF with document length normalization:
   weight(t) = IDF(t) × (tf × (k1 + 1)) / (tf + k1 × (1 - b + b × dl / avgdl))

src/text/tfidf/bm25.mbt::value:BM25Batch.name: BM25Batch
src/text/tfidf/bm25.mbt::value:BM25Batch.doc: Precomputed BM25 vectors for batch similarity calculation.

src/text/tfidf/bm25.mbt::value:from_toke.name: from_tokens
src/text/tfidf/bm25.mbt::value:from_toke.doc: Create a BM25Batch from pre-tokenized documents.

src/text/tfidf/bm25.mbt::value:length.name: length
src/text/tfidf/bm25.mbt::value:length.doc: Get the number of documents in the batch.

src/text/tfidf/bm25.mbt::value:similarit.name: similarity
src/text/tfidf/bm25.mbt::value:similarit.doc: Calculate similarity between two documents by index.

src/text/tfidf/bm25.mbt::value:all_pairs.name: all_pairs_above_threshold
src/text/tfidf/bm25.mbt::value:all_pairs.doc:
Calculate all pairwise similarities above a threshold.
 Uses inverted index posting lists for candidate pruning (same as TfidfBatch).

src/text/stats/threshold.mbt::value:knee.name: kneedle_threshold_index
src/text/stats/threshold.mbt::value:knee.doc:
Kneedle algorithm for finding the elbow (knee) point in a descending-sorted
 weight array. Returns the number of items before the knee — i.e., the count
 of "significant" items in the foreground class.

 For a decreasing curve, the knee is the point that deviates most below
 the straight line connecting the first and last points. This corresponds
 to the sharpest transition from high-weight signal to low-weight noise.

 The input array MUST be sorted in descending order.

 Reference: Satopaa et al., "Finding a 'Kneedle' in a Haystack", 2011.

src/filter/types.mbt::value:FilterConfig.name: FilterConfig
src/filter/types.mbt::value:FilterConfig.doc:
Filter configuration for file discovery.
 Pure data structure — no file I/O. Constructed by callers
 who load ignore patterns from appropriate sources.

src/filter/types.mbt::value:new.name: new
src/filter/types.mbt::value:new.doc: Create a new FilterConfig with default settings.

src/filter/types.mbt::value:with_user_pa.name: with_user_patterns
src/filter/types.mbt::value:with_user_pa.doc: Create a FilterConfig with user-specified include/exclude patterns.

src/filter/types.mbt::value:with_kgf_ign.name: with_kgf_ignore
src/filter/types.mbt::value:with_kgf_ign.doc: Add ignore patterns from a KGF spec.

src/filter/types.mbt::value:with_include.name: with_includes
src/filter/types.mbt::value:with_include.doc: Add include patterns to the config.

src/filter/types.mbt::value:with_exclude.name: with_excludes
src/filter/types.mbt::value:with_exclude.doc: Add exclude patterns to the config.

src/filter/types.mbt::value:with_gitigno.name: with_gitignore_from_dir
src/filter/types.mbt::value:with_gitigno.doc:
Load a gitignore-syntax file from a directory and merge into gitignore patterns.
 Callers must pass the filename from their domain's SoT constant.

src/filter/types.mbt::value:with_indexio.name: with_indexionignore_from_dir
src/filter/types.mbt::value:with_indexio.doc:
Load a gitignore-syntax file from a directory and merge into indexionignore patterns.
 Callers must pass the filename from their domain's SoT constant.

src/filter/types.mbt::value:is_included.name: is_included
src/filter/types.mbt::value:is_included.doc: Check if the result indicates the path should be included.

src/filter/defaults.mbt::value:default_e.name: default_exclude_patterns
src/filter/defaults.mbt::value:default_e.doc:
Minimal fallback exclude patterns.
 Language-specific patterns should be defined in KGF specs (=== ignore section).
 These are only applied when no KGF, .gitignore, or .indexionignore patterns apply.

src/filter/filter.mbt::value:should_incl.name: should_include
src/filter/filter.mbt::value:should_incl.doc:
Check if a path should be included based on filter configuration.
 Priority (highest to lowest):
 1. User --exclude (forces exclusion, highest priority)
 2. User --include (whitelist mode when specified)
 3. Project ignore file (".indexionignore")
 4. Git ignore file (".gitignore")
 5. KGF ignore patterns (language-specific)
 6. Default excludes (fallback)

src/filter/filter.mbt::value:should_skip.name: should_skip_dir
src/filter/filter.mbt::value:should_skip.doc:
Check if a directory should be skipped during traversal.
 This is used for early termination - if a directory is excluded,
 we don't need to traverse into it.
 Uses same priority as should_include but for directories only.

src/filter/filter.mbt::value:dir_could_c.name: dir_could_contain_pattern_match
src/filter/filter.mbt::value:dir_could_c.doc:
Check if a directory could contain files that match a path pattern.
 For pattern "src/filter/*.mbt":
   - dir "src" → true (prefix of pattern's directory)
   - dir "src/filter" → true (matches pattern's directory)
   - dir "src/core" → false (not on the path)

src/filter/filter.mbt::value:extract_pat.name: extract_pattern_prefix
src/filter/filter.mbt::value:extract_pat.doc:
Extract the fixed prefix of a glob pattern (before any wildcards).
 "src/filter/*.mbt" → "src/filter"
 "src/**/test.mbt" → "src"
 "*.mbt" → ""

edges.name: InitConfig
edges.doc: Configuration for init command.

edges.name: default
edges.doc: Create a default InitConfig with current directory as target.
Create InitConfig from argparse Matches.

edges.name: command
edges.doc: Define the init command for argparse.
Entry point for the init subcommand using argparse Matches.
edges.to: cmd/indexion/init/cli.mbt::doc::Entry point for the init subcommand using argparse Matches.

edges.name: KgfConfig
edges.doc: CLI configuration.
Create KgfConfig from argparse Matches.

edges.name: command
edges.doc: Define the kgf command for argparse.
Entry point for the kgf subcommand using argparse Matches.
edges.to: cmd/indexion/kgf/cli.mbt::doc::Entry point for the kgf subcommand using argparse Matches.

edges.doc: Update KGF specs from GitHub.
Add a single KGF spec from GitHub.
edges.to: cmd/indexion/kgf/cli.mbt::doc::Add a single KGF spec from GitHub.

edges.name: list_specs_json
edges.doc: List installed KGF specs as JSON string.

edges.name: all_specs_content
edges.doc:
Return a map of spec name -> spec content for all installed specs.
 Used by serve to expose /api/kgf/specs/{name} routes.
Resolve spec name from file path (shared resolution logic).
edges.to: cmd/indexion/kgf/cli.mbt::doc::Resolve spec name from file path (shared resolution logic).

edges.name: tokenize_json

edges.doc:
Count edges of built-in kinds whose endpoints are not registered as a
 module or symbol in the serialized graph. A non-zero count is a failure
 of the graph-structural completeness invariant this test suite exists to
 guard.

 The check runs against the round-tripped graph (to_json → from_json) so
 it observes the final output shape including any backfill the serializer
 performs. This matches what downstream consumers (the React viewer,
 IDE plugins, etc.) actually see.

edges.doc:

edges.name: LEAF_BASE_RADIUS
edges.doc:
Minimum radius of a leaf cluster (a single-node shell is about
 *  this wide). All sizing is relative to this.

edges.name: SIBLING_PADDING
edges.doc:
Extra world-units of padding between sibling clusters so they
 *  don't kiss.

edges.name: OWNERSHIP_SHELL_SCALE
edges.doc:
Radius granted to a mid-level path for its own owned nodes,
 *  relative to the sibling slot size.

edges.name: shellRadiusFor
edges.doc:
* Radius of a simple shell holding `count` nodes. Uses the sphere
 * packing rule r_shell ≈ LEAF_BASE_RADIUS · √count, softened at the
 * low end so a single node still occupies a visible area.

A small graph with folder-like node ids so the hierarchical path

edges.name: snapshotPositions

edges.name: byCommunity
edges.doc:
* Modularity-greedy community detection (Louvain phase 1).
 *
 * For each node, we compute the modularity gain from moving it into
 * each neighbouring community, and choose the best. Iterated until
 * stable. This typically produces balanced mid-sized communities
 * rather than one giant blob (which plain label propagation tends to).
 *
 * Modularity gain formula (undirected):
 *   ΔQ = [ k_in / m ]  −  [ Σ_tot · k / (2 m²) ]
 * where k_in is the sum of edge weights from the node to nodes already
 * in the target community, Σ_tot is the total edge weight incident on
 * the target community, k is the degree of the node, and m is the
 * total edge weight in the graph.

edges.name: ViewNode
edges.doc:
* A node in the view graph. Identity fields are readonly; simulation
 * fields (position, velocity, pinned) are mutable for in-place update.
 *
 * The placement model is 3D. The 2D renderer projects (x,y,z) to the
 * screen using parallel projection, mapping z to size and opacity.
 * A future WebGL renderer can read the same (x,y,z) directly.

edges.name: ViewEdge
edges.doc: Directed edge between two view nodes.

edges.name: ViewGraph
edges.doc: The unified graph model consumed by simulation, renderer, and interaction.

 * in useEffect. The effect only initiates the fetch (side effect); the
 * cache notifies subscribers when the result is available.

edges.name: colorForKind
edges.doc:
* FNV-1a hash → deterministic HSL color for a token kind.
 *
 * Dark theme:  lightness 55-70%, saturation 55-80%  (bright on dark bg)
 * Light theme: lightness 25-40%, saturation 55-80%  (dark on light bg)
 *
 * Hue and saturation are identical across themes so the same token kind
 * keeps its colour identity; only lightness is flipped.

edges.name: warned
edges.doc:
* @file KGF tokenizer — stub implementation.
 *
 * This is the default export when `moon build --target js` hasn't been run.
 * Returns an empty token array, causing syntax highlighting to fall through
 * to plain text rendering.
 *
 * When the MoonBit build artifact exists, consumer bundlers should alias
 * `@indexion/kgf-tokenizer` to the artifact path to get real tokenization:
 *
 *   _build/js/debug/build/cmd/kgf-tokenizer/kgf-tokenizer.js
 *
 * The MoonBit source lives at `cmd/kgf-tokenizer/main.mbt` and exports
 * the same `tokenize(spec_text, source) -> JSON string` signature.

edges.name: tokenize
edges.doc:
* Tokenize source code using a KGF spec string.
 * Returns a JSON string: `[{"kind":"...","text":"...","pos":N}, ...]`

edges.name: DiscoverOptions
edges.doc: Options for file discovery.

edges.name: SupportedFile
edges.doc:
SoT: `path` is always a real filesystem path.
 Archive files are represented as a single entry with concatenated content.
 Virtual paths (archive-internal) never appear here — they are handled
 by `@common.collect_files` for per-inner-file expansion.

edges.name: default
edges.doc: Create default discover options.
Create discover options.

edges.name: root_dir_from_paths
edges.doc:
Derive root_dir from a list of target paths.
 Uses the first path, falling back to ".".
 SoT for root_dir derivation — all callers that have paths but not
 an explicit root_dir should use this instead of inline logic.

edges.name: discover_files
edges.doc:
Collect all file paths from given input paths.
 Supports glob patterns (e.g., "src/**/*.mbt").
 Respects includes, excludes, and recursion settings.
 Loads project-level ignore files from root_dir if specified.

Load supported file metadata from an explicit path list.

 SoT guarantee: every `SupportedFile.path` is a real filesystem path.
 Archive files (content_type: archive) are expanded via VFS, and their
 text content is concatenated into a single entry keyed by the archive's
 real path. This ensures downstream code can safely perform `@fs.*`
 operations on `SupportedFile.path` without encountering virtual paths.

 For per-inner-file archive expansion (needed by explore/search/grep),
 use `@common.collect_files` with a KGF registry instead.
edges.to:
src/pipeline/discover.mbt::doc::Load supported file metadata from an explicit path list.

 SoT guarantee: every `SupportedFile.path` is a real filesystem path.
 Archive files (content_type: archive) are expanded via VFS, and their
 text content is concatenated into a single entry keyed by the archive's
 real path. This ensures downstream code can safely perform `@fs.*`
 operations on `SupportedFile.path` without encountering virtual paths.

 For per-inner-file archive expansion (needed by explore/search/grep),
 use `@common.collect_files` with a KGF registry instead.

Use MinHash + LSH to find candidate pairs with approximate Jaccard similarity.
 Near-linear time complexity: O(N × num_hashes × bands) for indexing,
 versus O(N²) for brute-force or inverted-index approaches.
 Takes (display_name, content) pairs. Returns (i, j) where i < j.

edges.name: apply_fdr_correction
edges.doc:
Apply Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction to batch comparison results.

 `alpha` is the target FDR level (e.g., 0.05 for 5% expected false discoveries).
 `total_pairs` is the total number of pairs tested (N*(N-1)/2), needed
 to account for pairs that fell below the initial threshold.
 `threshold` is the similarity threshold that was used to produce the matches.

 Returns filtered matches that survive FDR correction.

edges.name: apply_fdr
edges.doc:
Apply FDR correction to a BatchResult, returning a new BatchResult
 with only statistically significant matches.

 `alpha` is the target FDR level.
 `threshold` is the similarity threshold used to generate the matches.
 If not provided, the minimum observed score is used.

Run batch comparison using BM25 strategy.

Run batch comparison using Jensen-Shannon Divergence.

Run pairwise comparison with pre-filtering.

 and MinHash+LSH for large sets.

edges.name: ClassifyModel
edges.doc:
ClassifyModel represents a trained classification model persisted in
 a KGF `=== classify` section.

 This is NOT a hand-authored vocabulary list. The weights are computed
 from a document corpus by a training process (e.g., `indexion classify train`)
 and serialized into the KGF spec for later inference.

 ## Model structure

 A model consists of:
 - **Metadata**: model type, training parameters, corpus statistics
 - **Classes**: named classification categories, each with:
   - A prior log-probability (base rate from training corpus)
   - A vocabulary of terms with conditional log-probabilities P(term|class)

 ## Inference

 Given a text to classify, a consumer:
 1. Tokenizes the text into terms
 2. For each class, sums the prior + Σ log P(term|class) for observed terms
 3. The class with the highest posterior score wins

 The model is agnostic to what the classes represent — normative vs. meta,
 requirement vs. boilerplate, etc. Class semantics are determined by the
 KGF spec author and the training corpus.

edges.name: ClassifyClass
edges.doc: A single classification class with its prior and vocabulary.

edges.name: get_meta
edges.doc: Returns a metadata value by key, or None.

edges.name: get_class_names
edges.doc: Returns all class names.

edges.name: get_class
edges.doc: Returns a class definition by name.

edges.name: is_empty
edges.doc: Returns true if this model has no classes.

edges.name: classify
edges.doc:
Classify a tokenized text by computing posterior scores for each class.
 Returns an array of (class_name, score) pairs sorted by descending score.

 The score for each class is: prior + Σ vocab[term] for observed terms.
 Terms not in the class vocabulary contribute 0 (equivalent to uniform
 smoothing at the training-time default).

edges.name: predict
edges.doc:
Returns the predicted class name (highest posterior score).
 Returns None if the model has no classes.

edges.name: term_weight
edges.doc:
Returns the conditional log-probability of a term in this class.
 Returns 0.0 for unknown terms (smoothed default).

edges.name: KGFSpec
edges.doc:
KGFSpec represents the complete KGF specification for a language.

 Sections are parsed lazily: the raw text for each === section is stored
 at construction time and the heavy parsing (lex patterns, grammar rules,
 semantic blocks, etc.) is deferred until the first accessor call.
 Once parsed, results are memoized in the corresponding cache field.

 Phase 1 (eager, at registry load):
   language, resolver, features, ignore
 Phase 2 (lazy, on first access):
   tokens, keyword_kinds        — lex section (pattern compilation is heavy)
   rules, attrs                 — grammar + attrs sections
   semantics                    — semantics section
   preprocess, preprocess_fst   — preprocess sections

Creates a new KGFSpec with all sections eagerly populated (legacy path).
 Used by tests and code that already has parsed results.
edges.to:
src/kgf/types/spec.mbt::doc::Creates a new KGFSpec with all sections eagerly populated (legacy path).
 Used by tests and code that already has parsed results.

edges.doc:
Creates a KGFSpec with deferred section parsing.

 The raw section texts are stored for lazy parsing on first access.

edges.doc:
Detect proxy functions from tokenized source code.
 A proxy function has a body that consists solely of a single function call
 forwarding its parameters without additional logic.

 Set `include_self_delegation` or `include_bare_constructors` to include them.

edges.name: parse_classify
edges.doc:
Parses the `=== classify` section of a KGF spec.

 The classify section contains a trained classification model persisted
 by a training tool (e.g., `indexion classify train`). The weights are
 computed from a document corpus, NOT hand-authored.

 ## Syntax

 ```
 # Metadata: key = value (model parameters, corpus statistics)
 model_type = naive_bayes
 corpus_size = 150
 smoothing = laplace

 # Class definition: @class_name prior=log_probability
 # Followed by indented term=weight lines (conditional log-probabilities)
 @normative prior=-0.693
   shall=-0.105
   must=-0.223
   required=-0.511

 @meta prior=-1.204
   introduction=-0.693
   scope=-1.099
 ```

 Lines starting with `#` are comments. Blank lines are ignored.

edges.name: sub_tokenize_vocab

Split text into lowercase words and emit "vocab:" prefixed feature strings.
 Words shorter than 3 characters are discarded.

 Used for vocabulary_token_kinds content (doc comments, string literals,
 natural language text) to enable vocabulary-level comparison.

Tokenize a file into feature strings using KGF.

 Produces two layers:
 1. KGF tokens → "Kind:value" (identifiers) or "Kind" (syntax)
 2. vocabulary_token_kinds → "vocab:word" sub-tokens

 For document specs with `embedded_code_fence` / `embedded_content_token`
 features, embedded code blocks are re-tokenized with the detected
 language's KGF spec, producing the same feature strings as standalone
 files. Reference tokens (e.g., INLINE_CODE) emit their captured value
 as identifier feature strings.

 Feature strings are lowercased; those shorter than 2 characters are discarded.
edges.to:
src/kgf/tokenize/tokenize.mbt::doc::Tokenize a file into feature strings using KGF.

 Produces two layers:
 1. KGF tokens → "Kind:value" (identifiers) or "Kind" (syntax)
 2. vocabulary_token_kinds → "vocab:word" sub-tokens

 For document specs with `embedded_code_fence` / `embedded_content_token`
 features, embedded code blocks are re-tokenized with the detected
 language's KGF spec, producing the same feature strings as standalone
 files. Reference tokens (e.g., INLINE_CODE) emit their captured value
 as identifier feature strings.

 Feature strings are lowercased; those shorter than 2 characters are discarded.

edges.name: LanguageToolkit
edges.doc:
LanguageToolkit caches all runtime artifacts derived from a KGFSpec.

 Creating a Lexer, building feature sets (identifier kinds, vocabulary kinds,
 qualifier separators, etc.) from spec features — these are pure derivations
 that never change for a given spec. Rather than letting each consumer rebuild
 them on every file, LanguageToolkit constructs them once and exposes the
 cached results.

 Obtain a toolkit via `ToolkitRegistry::get(lang)` or the convenience
 `ToolkitRegistry::for_path(path)`.

Build a LanguageToolkit from a KGFSpec.
 All derived artifacts are computed eagerly at construction time, so
 subsequent access is zero-cost.
edges.to:
src/kgf/toolkit/toolkit.mbt::doc::Build a LanguageToolkit from a KGFSpec.
 All derived artifacts are computed eagerly at construction time, so
 subsequent access is zero-cost.

edges.name: to_json_string
edges.doc:
Convert CodeGraph to JSON string following KGF format.
 Format:
 {
   "modules": { "id": { "file": "..." } },
   "symbols": { "id": { "name": "...", "kind": "...", "ns": "...", "module": "..." } },
   "edges": [{ "kind": "...", "from": "...", "to": "...", ...attrs }]
 }

 Before writing, edge endpoints are reconciled against the node tables:
 - `declares` edges whose `to` does not match a symbol ID are remapped to the
   canonical symbol by `(from, name)` lookup (KGF scripts often emit
   `<file>::<name>` while `attrs def` registers `<file>::<ns>:<name>`).
 - Any `to` that still has no matching node is registered as an external
   module with `file: null` so consumers see every endpoint in `modules` or
   `symbols`.

edges.name: compress
edges.doc:
Compress text using LZ77-style algorithm with hash chain acceleration.
 Uses an 8KB sliding window with trigram hash chains to find matches efficiently.
 The output format:
 - Literal byte: 0x00 followed by the byte
 - Back reference: 0x01 followed by 2-byte offset and 1-byte length

edges.name: compressed_size
edges.doc: Get the compressed size of text in bytes.

edges.name: ncd
edges.doc:
Calculate Normalized Compression Distance between two texts.
 NCD(x, y) = (C(xy) - min(C(x), C(y))) / max(C(x), C(y))

 Uses LZ77-style compression to measure text similarity.
 Similar texts compress better together (lower NCD), while different texts
 do not benefit from concatenation (higher NCD).

 Returns NCD value between 0 (identical) and ~1 (completely different).

edges.name: calculate_adjacent_ncd
edges.doc:
Calculate NCD values for sliding window of adjacent texts.
 For texts [a, b, c, d], calculates:
 - ncd(a, b)
 - ncd(b, c)
 - ncd(c, d)

 Returns array of NCD values (length = texts.length - 1).

edges.name: min_max_normalize
edges.doc:
Normalize an array of values using Min-Max normalization.
 Returns values in range [0, 1].
 If all values are the same, returns 0.5 for all.

 WARNING: Min-Max is sensitive to outliers. Prefer `robust_normalize`
 for signals that may contain extreme values.

edges.name: robust_normalize
edges.doc:
Normalize an array of values using robust z-score (median + MAD).
 Resistant to outliers unlike Min-Max normalization.
 Output is shifted to [0, 1] range via sigmoid-like clamping.
 If MAD is zero (all values equal or nearly so), returns 0.5 for all.

edges.name: reciprocal_rank_fusion
edges.doc:
Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) for combining multiple ranked signal arrays.
 Each input array is ranked independently, then fused via:
   score(i) = sum_j( 1 / (k + rank_j(i)) )
 where k is a smoothing constant (typically 60).
 Returns fused scores normalized to [0, 1].

 All input arrays must have the same length. Higher values = higher rank.

edges.name: find_local_maxima
edges.doc:
Find local maxima indices where values exceed the threshold.
 Uses moving average smoothing (radius=1) to reduce noise, then filters
 by peak prominence to reject insignificant local bumps.
 A peak must satisfy:
   1. Smoothed value >= threshold
   2. Is a local maximum in the smoothed signal
   3. Peak prominence >= 20% of the signal range (adaptive)

edges.doc:

 Returns divergence values and their corresponding positions.
edges.to:
src/segmentation/window/divergence.mbt::doc::Calculate TF-IDF divergence between adjacent sentence windows.
 Returns divergence values and their corresponding positions.

edges.doc:
Calculate combined NCD+TF-IDF divergence between adjacent sentence windows.
 Uses Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) to combine NCD and TF-IDF signals.
 RRF is rank-based, so it is invariant to scale differences between signals
 and robust to outliers in either signal.
edges.to:
src/segmentation/window/divergence.mbt::doc::Calculate combined NCD+TF-IDF divergence between adjacent sentence windows.
 Uses Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) to combine NCD and TF-IDF signals.
 RRF is rank-based, so it is invariant to scale differences between signals
 and robust to outliers in either signal.

edges.doc:
Parameters:
 - sentences: Array of sentences to analyze
 - window_size: Number of sentences per window
 - ncd_weight: When <= 0, NCD is skipped (TF-IDF only). Otherwise both signals are fused via RRF.
 - tfidf_weight: When <= 0, TF-IDF is skipped (NCD only). Otherwise both signals are fused via RRF.

 Returns divergence values and their corresponding positions.
edges.to:

 - ncd_weight: When <= 0, NCD is skipped (TF-IDF only). Otherwise both signals are fused via RRF.
 - tfidf_weight: When <= 0, TF-IDF is skipped (NCD only). Otherwise both signals are fused via RRF.

 Returns divergence values and their corresponding positions.

edges.name: resolve_provider
edges.doc:
Resolve an EmbeddingProvider from named CLI/config parameters.

 provider_name: "openai" | "tfidf" | "auto" | ""
 api_key_env:   environment variable name for the API key
 base_url:      API base URL
 model:         embedding model name
 dim:           embedding dimension

 Rules:
 - "tfidf": always returns TfIdf(dim).
 - "openai": requires the env var to be set; returns Err if missing.
 - "auto" / "": uses OpenAI if the env var is set, TfIdf otherwise.
 - Unknown name: returns Err.

 Returns a map from line number to role string.
edges.to:
src/spec/align/spec_adapter.mbt::doc::Extract section roles from interpreted document facts.
 Returns a map from line number to role string.

edges.doc:
Determine whether a section should be included as a requirement candidate.

 Uses a three-tier classification strategy:

 1. **KGF document_section_role** (from semantics): If the spec emits
    explicit role annotations, use them. Roles "meta", "reference",
    "example", "appendix_code", "index" are excluded.

 2. **KGF classify model** (from `=== classify` section): If the spec
    has a trained classification model, use it to predict the section class.
    The predicted class determines inclusion/exclusion.

 3. **No classification data**: If neither role nor model is available,
    include the section (no filtering).
edges.to:
src/spec/align/spec_adapter.mbt::doc::Determine whether a section should be included as a requirement candidate.

 Uses a three-tier classification strategy:

 1. **KGF document_section_role** (from semantics): If the spec emits
    explicit role annotations, use them. Roles "meta", "reference",
    "example", "appendix_code", "index" are excluded.

 2. **KGF classify model** (from `=== classify` section): If the spec
    has a trained classification model, use it to predict the section class.
    The predicted class determines inclusion/exclusion.

 3. **No classification data**: If neither role nor model is available,
    include the section (no filtering).

edges.doc:
Returns true if the spec defines explicit requirement extraction rules
 (i.e., has semantic blocks that emit `document_requirement` notes).

 When such a spec finds 0 requirements in the primary path for a given
 file, the fallback section-scanning path is skipped — the spec defines
 what a requirement looks like, and none were found in this file.

 Specs like rfc-plaintext or technical-document do NOT define requirement
 extraction, so they rely entirely on the fallback. This function
 distinguishes the two cases by inspecting the spec's semantic definitions.
edges.to:
src/spec/align/spec_adapter.mbt::doc::Returns true if the spec defines explicit requirement extraction rules

 When such a spec finds 0 requirements in the primary path for a given
 file, the fallback section-scanning path is skipped — the spec defines
 what a requirement looks like, and none were found in this file.

 Specs like rfc-plaintext or technical-document do NOT define requirement
 extraction, so they rely entirely on the fallback. This function
 distinguishes the two cases by inspecting the spec's semantic definitions.

edges.doc:
Exact upper bound on unit-cost tree_similarity based on sizes alone.

 With CostConfig::default() (all costs = 1.0):
   raw_dist >= |size1 - size2|
   max_dist = size1 + size2
   similarity <= 1 - |size1-size2| / (size1+size2)

edges.doc:

edges.name: calculate_tsed_indexed
edges.doc:
Calculate TSED similarity on pre-indexed trees.
 Returns a value in [0, 1] where 1 = identical trees.

edges.name: tsed_similarity_upper_bound
edges.doc:
Exact upper bound on TSED similarity based on sizes alone.

 With max-based normalization:
   best case dist = max_size - min_size
   upper_bound = 1 - (max_size - min_size) / max_size = min_size / max_size

edges.name: calculate_tsed
edges.doc:
Calculate TSED similarity between two trees.
 Returns a value in [0, 1] where 1 = identical trees.

 Normalization: max(1 - dist / max(size1, size2), 0)

 This normalization ensures:
 - Identical trees → 1.0 (regardless of size)
 - All labels different, same structure → 0.0
 - No magic number penalties

edges.name: calculate_tsed_distance
edges.doc:
Calculate TSED distance between two trees.
 Returns 1 - similarity, a value in [0, 1] where 0 = identical trees.

 Returns true if comparison should proceed.

edges.doc:
# MCP Streamable HTTP transport

 Serves MCP over HTTP using the Streamable HTTP transport defined
 in the MCP 2025-03-26 specification.

 Endpoint: POST /mcp
 Content-Type: application/json
 Response: application/json (single JSON-RPC response)

 Also supports:
 - GET /mcp for SSE (server-sent events) — future extension
 - DELETE /mcp for session termination — future extension
edges.to:
src/mcp/transport/http.mbt::doc::# MCP Streamable HTTP transport

 Serves MCP over HTTP using the Streamable HTTP transport defined
 in the MCP 2025-03-26 specification.

 Endpoint: POST /mcp
 Content-Type: application/json
 Response: application/json (single JSON-RPC response)

 Also supports:
 - GET /mcp for SSE (server-sent events) — future extension
 - DELETE /mcp for session termination — future extension

edges.doc:
Check 1: Broken internal links.
 Scan page content for wiki:// links where the target page ID does not exist.
 Links inside backtick code spans (e.g. `wiki://page-id`) are skipped.
edges.to:
src/docgen/wiki/lint/lint.mbt::doc::Check 1: Broken internal links.
 Scan page content for wiki:// links where the target page ID does not exist.
 Links inside backtick code spans (e.g. `wiki://page-id`) are skipped.

edges.doc:
Check 2: Orphan pages.
 Pages not reachable from the navigation tree and not linked by any other page.
edges.to:
src/docgen/wiki/lint/lint.mbt::doc::Check 2: Orphan pages.
 Pages not reachable from the navigation tree and not linked by any other page.

edges.doc:
Check 3: Missing cross-references.
 Pages referencing the same source files but not linking to each other.
edges.to:
src/docgen/wiki/lint/lint.mbt::doc::Check 3: Missing cross-references.
 Pages referencing the same source files but not linking to each other.

edges.doc:
Check 4: Stale source references.
 Pages whose sources point to files that no longer exist on disk.
 Source paths are resolved relative to the project root derived from wiki_dir.
edges.to:
src/docgen/wiki/lint/lint.mbt::doc::Check 4: Stale source references.
 Pages whose sources point to files that no longer exist on disk.
 Source paths are resolved relative to the project root derived from wiki_dir.

edges.doc:
Check 5: Empty pages.
 Pages with no meaningful content.
edges.to:
src/docgen/wiki/lint/lint.mbt::doc::Check 5: Empty pages.
 Pages with no meaningful content.

edges.doc:
Check 6: Manifest-file mismatch.
 Pages in wiki.json without corresponding .md files, or .md files not in manifest.
edges.to:
src/docgen/wiki/lint/lint.mbt::doc::Check 6: Manifest-file mismatch.
 Pages in wiki.json without corresponding .md files, or .md files not in manifest.

edges.name: WikiSearchIndex
edges.doc:
Wiki search index — wraps SearchEngine with wiki-specific metadata.

 provider: the EmbeddingProvider used at build time.
   Stored here so search() and update_page() use the same provider.

 section_to_vid: section_id → vcdb VectorId.
   Used for partial updates: remove old vectors before inserting new ones.

 next_vid: monotonically increasing counter for new vector IDs.
   Persisted in search-sections.json so load restores it.

edges.name: section_id_attr
edges.doc: vcdb attr key used to store section_id on each vector.

Build a search index from wiki pages.
 vid is assigned monotonically from 1; next_vid is persisted for partial updates.

 vid is assigned monotonically from 1; next_vid is persisted for partial updates.

edges.doc:
Update a single page's sections in the index.
 Removes old vectors, embeds new sections in one batch, inserts them.
 The TF-IDF vocabulary is NOT rebuilt — queries use the existing vocabulary.
 For vocabulary drift after many updates, rebuild with `wiki index build --full`.
edges.to:
src/docgen/wiki/search/search.mbt::doc::Update a single page's sections in the index.
 Removes old vectors, embeds new sections in one batch, inserts them.
